name: WILLIAM BRYAN ALMON, B.S.C.
motto: "To be honest as this world goes, is to be one man picked out of ten thousand."
description: Do you remember the commercial looking guy with the derbies and army shoes, soliciting trade on the campus last fall? Well, sir! This is none other than he. Almon came here from Bordeaux with the other fellows to help thwart the Kaiser's plans. Listen, I'll tell you about "Bryan," fellows. He's from Heard County. "Deep Heard," so "Fish" Ware says. Although you don't see so much of Mr. Almon in a Teahoundish sort of way, he's a Devil with the women. He is a three-year man and a hard boner. He keeps a light in his Candler Hall window long after less studious boys are in bed. He is a commercial student. He used to affirm that he was going west when he finished. Whenever he goes, he'll make good. Gentlemen, he's a man.

name: HERBERT GLENN BAILEY, D.V.M.
motto: "A horse is valuable only when broke—how different with a man."
description: "Bailey" is one of the Veterinary Medicine sharks, and we add right here that we can hardly refer to him without using his "Veterinary" nickname, and that we only refrain because of the fact that "orders are orders," and we believe in obeying them at times. "Bailey" is an excellent student, quiet and good-natured in his bearing, with a smile for everybody, especially the Co-eds. Also he seems to be superendowed with that greatest of all gifts, good common "horse" sense. This is no pun upon his knowledge of the equine species. He possesses a good store of general information and consequently forms a valuable asset to all stag parties and is often the central figure in campus "bull" sessions. We expect you to push yourself to the forefront out in the world of hard knocks. "Bailey," because of those sterling qualities of genuine worth which have placed you in the front ranks of our class.

name: MERWIN CLIFFORD BAILEY, B.S.C.E.
high school: Third District A. and M. School of Americus
motto: "Have you thought of the good that could have been done had you not put off starting till tomorrow."
description: Not being old enough to join that memorable "gang" of S. A. T. C. boys, he decided to become a disciple of "Little" Charlie. Though quiet and unassuming, he is a man of great possibilities, and, outside of several visits to the Dean, the military officials and his professor's offices, "M. C." has enjoyed a very successful and progressive college career. He has derived great pleasure and spent most of his pastime in constant visits to St. Mary's and to "Caspers." One of his noblest characteristics and greatest assets is his loyalty to his friends. Indeed, a truer friend hath no man. We understand he is going to India, or South America, upon graduation, to practice his profession. Our best wishes go with him for wonderful success and happiness in life.

name: PAUL THEODORE BARRETT, A.B.S.S.
motto: "Experience is a great University which offers a variety of courses."
description: "P. T." is one of those steady, quiet, unassuming fellows who can be depended upon on all occasions. He doesn't shout his abilities or acclaim his friendships on the street corners, but he can always be counted on as a hard worker, a producer of the goods, and a loyal friend. He came to Georgia five years ago from Commerce High, not a stranger, for Commerce is our next door neighbor and has sent us many good men. Although his college course was interrupted by two years' absence, he accomplished the hard task of finishing in three years. We understand that he is going to teach, and also take up the study of law. The best of luck to you, "P. T.," in your preceptorial and forensic undertakings. Remember, too, that you are gifted with that pleasing personality that girls cannot withstand. We know Cupid has great things in store for you. And when the question of a college arises, "P. T.," you will not forget your Alma Mater to whom you have been so loyal.

name: FRANCIS MARO BIRD, A.B.
motto: "May our lives be untainted by things frivolous and vulgar, but give enjoyment in what is noblest and best."
description: When "Buster" came to the University four years ago, he looked like a small chap to be entering college. But the old saying goes that gold is tied up in the smallest packages. "Buster" always holds a strong hand with the weaker sex and if he can decide which one, we are afraid that he will enter a broader field than a college course. "Buster" waited until this year to go out for the Glee Club, but made it with ease. When he went he said that he wanted Caruso to be out of his way before he tried out his art as a singer. "Buster" is a very likeable fellow and has made many friends since entering college. He stays to the end on anything that he attempts to do, and we know that this will be one of his characteristics when he gets out into the world for himself. Luck to you, "Buster."

name: JOEL CONYERS BENNETT, B.S.A.
motto: "Some take all but I leave none."
description: One of Atlanta's social leaders, and he has represented her well. He is not only a good leader and a good student, but also a good fellow. "Conyers" is the type of man that everybody likes. He came to us with a silent smile and an ambition, and he leaves us as a man upon whose shoulders any responsibility could rest, and a man of whom our state shall some day be proud. While with us he has made many friends, both male and female. We hate to see you leave, "Conyers," because you have won a place in our hearts that will be hard for another to fill. "Conyers" has featured the social festivities. He can be seen almost any afternoon practicing some new step to show the visitors for the next big week-end. May you live long and happily and never grow too old to enjoy the return to a Georgia Commencement.

name: Samuel Means Boney
motto: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: Boney, a native of the Palmetto state, came to us from the Citadel, and after completing two years' work, he is claimed by Georgia as one of her sons. His popularity is due to his many qualities,—his unassuming manners, ready smile, his quick wit, all contributing. As an all-round athlete there are few men who excel Sam. As to his football ability, he is a fast man, a vicious tackler, a heady player, where true worth is recognized by all. On the basketball court, he is a fast dribbler, and a quick thinker, who knows every phase of the game. Boney, never seeking glory, has achieved it—his distinction lying in his unique success, for he has lived clean, made a multitude of friends and is always ready to serve one. These qualities of genuine success, so forcibly demonstrated in school, assure him of a brilliant future.

name: Jesse Brenard Bookhardt
motto: "It's not who you are, but what you do, that counts."
description: J. B. is probably the youngest member of the class, but it has been said that, "Out of the mouths of babies cometh forth wisdom," and we find it that way with him. He has wisdom far beyond his years. Since coming among us, "Jack" has made many fast friends. The highest hope one can make for his future is that he may be as successful hereafter in the making of friends, as he has been in the past. He has become a Vamp in his stay at Georgia, and bids fair to be a regular "Devil" when he gets back to Powder Springs. It is said that he specialized in Ag. Engineering so that he could lay off the streets of his home town on the contour system. His knowledge of Engineering should fit him to be a good road foreman back in Cobb County. Jesse, you have endeared yourself to the hearts of the students. We are sorry to see you leave. Good luck to you.

name: MACK ELMER BRAND, B.S.A.
motto: "Knowledge is better than weapons of war."
clubs: Treasurer of Saddle and Sirloin Club
description: Four years ago Loganville sent a regular regiment of her sons over to the University. All of them have made good records while here, and "Judge" is not an exception. He is a seclusive fellow, apparently caring little for the more boisterous side of college life. He is studious and his fine standing as a scholar is the result. "Judge" is always careful with his tongue and has never been known to make hasty statements which would later be regretted. He is beginning to get a little restless, however, as is indicated by his numerous excursions out among the Co-eds. We believe that another year here would rob him entirely of the reputation which he has built up. But then a man's a college senior only once, so let him have his turn. Mack is scheduled to become a professor, and if he does, we believe that he will make a success in his life work.

name: JOHN FRANKLIN BRANNEN, JR., B.S.A.
motto: "You do not have to fight or struggle, you only have to know."
clubs: Secretary and President of Agricultural Club
description: Halt! Pass no further until you have read of the noble exploits of this knight from the plains of Bulloch, and the alleys of Statesboro. Four years ago this member of the Brannen tribe came forth and enlisted in the services of his Uncle Samuel as a sailor, to sail the wide, wide seas. After his enlistment his unit sailed, amid the weeping and gnashing of teeth, to Lucas Hill, where the anchor was dropped until the war was over. Being free and having nothing to do, "J. F." sailed over across Tanyard branch to do homage at the throne of King Andy. In the Ag. college he has become one of the big mugs and has often swayed the great unwashed by his masterful eloquence! John has made a success of his college days in politics, debating, and otherwise, and we predict for him even greater successes in after life.

name: THOMAS JACKSON BRIGHTWELL, B.S.C.
motto: "Speak only when you know whereof you speak."
description: "Tom" entered the University in the winter of 1919. Since then he has been among us almost continually, except for time spent in short excursions back to the "home fires" at Maxeys. Because of his many "hail-fellow-well-met" qualities, Tom has made numerous friends in divers University circles. As to his love affairs, well, they have been rather limited. (Limited to Georgia). Besides these convivial tendencies, "Tom" has applied a good deal of his time to his work and his success in Academic circles is evidenced by the long list of scholastic honors which are his. These successes and the fact that he has always stood for what is right and worth while in college life predict a brilliant future for him. The best wishes of his many sincere friends go with him now as we say "Bon voyage, 'Tom'."

name: BENJAMIN JAMES BROACH, B.S.
motto: "Life is too short to be complete, therefore, make good your days of youth."
enrolltime: finished here in the short time of three years
description: "Ben" finished here in the short time of three years. He is one of the most conscientious workers in the University. If "Ben" tells you that he is going to do anything you can count on it to the limit. "Ben" first started out in Washington and Lee University. But he soon found out his mistake and came to Georgia. "Ben" is rather timid when it comes to telling you where he lives, he may and will tell you he gets his mail from one town and lives in the other. But if all of the representatives of Point Peter are like "Ben," then we are satisfied that the town is all right. "Ben" went out for track this year and the hopes of the captain and coach rose very much. They thought that they had another Paddock. He always makes a hit with the ladies and that is about his only fault. "Ben" will make good, we know. Here's to you.

name: GEORGE MARION BROADHURST, B.S.C.
motto: "If you want to sell more goods, learn more reasons why people should buy them."
description: "Chancellor" is one of the old stand-bys of the Commerce Department, who by zealous application to his studies and his profession has developed into an excellent business man. He has won and is still winning "his spurs" in his own line of endeavor and has equally well won a warm place in the hearts of those who know him. And too, we will add here that he has seemingly won more than a warm place in the hearts of some of the Co-eds, one in particular; but we do not marvel at this at all, for how could it be otherwise when one possesses such a captivating smile and such a winning personality? "Chancellor" has an excellent habit of paddling his own canoe and leaving the other man to paddle his, and as long as this is his practical philosophy of life, we can augur for him only happiness, peace and success.

name: MARION JACK BRODNAX, B.S.C.
motto: "Defeat is only for those who accept it."
clubs: General Staff, R. O. T. C.
description: He followed his older brother to school one day—that's why he's come to Georgia. Seriously, he came in the autumn of 1918 to enlist under the colors of the S. A. T. C. Twice he fought by our side in the battles of Lucas Hill, but we could not keep him there for they knighted him with the shevrons of a sergeant and from that day to this he's walked an easy road. Fair and tall, a type of the true Anglo-Saxon, he towers over the heads of most of us and is renowned for his amiableness with the ladies. Some say he desperately loves a maiden, but we are skeptical on that point. However, this we know—he receives an average of six and two-fifths special deliveries per diem. Does that sound like love? Marion has many times more friends than he is inches tall. He is quite a few inches tall. All of these friends wish for him the success and happiness which his earnestness and industry deserves. "To know him is to love him."

name: DAVID WILLIAM BROOKS, B.S.A.
motto: "Work not for yourself but always strive onward that you may be able to pull another up."
clubs: Sophomore and Junior Championship Basketball Teams
description: Young men! Will you kindly cease firing a moment while I tell you of "Jimmie" Brooks, the notorious pedagogue from Royston? He entered Georgia in the fall of '18 and became a member of the famous fighting cadet corps. "Jimmie's" idea of college in his freshman year was that it was a place of rest and not of study. Although he made fair grades that year they were low enough to cause him trouble trying to raise them during his second and third sessions. He finished his degree requirements last summer and has spent his fourth year teaching and taking some graduate work. He has been an excellent student and should he go into the teaching profession, his experience here would insure him success. However, we think he will go back to Royston and open a grocery, for which work he is admirably fitted. So long, "Jimmie," luck to you!

name: GUILFORD McGHEE CANNON, B.S.
motto: "Not too serious, not too gay, but a jolly good fellow."
description: "Rudolph Vaselino" Cannon, III, hails from the mountains of far North Georgia, where they use possums for watch dogs and owls for chickens. Since entering the University some three years ago Guilford has divided his time into three equal parts, books, a motor cycle, and a girl. He has been fairly successful in the first two lines of activities, but the result of the third is yet to be seen. He is a gentleman of poise and grace, is Guilford, and we have no doubt that he will bring enlightenment and culture to the sturdy yeomanry of the township of Dalton. We wit also, that he will create a devastation of hearts among the females of said township. Good luck to you, Guilford. May you live long, love well, and battle Father Time into a ripe old age.

name: HOMER GERALD CARREKER, A.B.
motto: "Life has its hills, its valleys, its ups, its downs. I'm taking it as it comes, reserving kicks for myself."
description: Did you ever meet a happy-go-lucky chap that just winks his eye, and laughs at the worst as well as the best? Well, that's "Jazz" all over. You can search the seven seas for a man with such a winning chuckle and not find him. You've simply got to feel at home around him. No one who ever met "Jazz" Carreker will ever forget him. Those who know him love him, and those who are fortunate enough to be among his friends bear witness that he wove his way into their lives and became permanently a part of their patterns. "Jazz" is one of the men whom everybody likes without knowing just why. We have decided that it is no single quality, but the entire wealth of his lovable and striking personality that draws us to him. "Jazz" is more than square with all men, and we will carry to our graves the cherished memory of his friendship.

name: BRIGGS CARSON, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "'Tis wise to think what you speak, rather than at all times speak what you think."
description: What manner of man is this, who turneth troubles aside and continueth along his way, as merrily as ever before? Gentlemen, he is the embodiment of speed as that quality applies to the cinder path and affairs of the heart. Over two years ago when our subject first whizzed into Athens the girls emitted piercing screams and jumped from pillar to post in high glee. "Here," they said, "is our ideal." Bring forth the royal diadem, sister designers, and he shall have even Sunday night closed dates. My word, the lad hath a gullible appearance, what? Briggs fooled them, and today picks nonchalantly his favored from the ranks of the "frails." Our hero is a speculative sort of chap, and stands right jealously behind his conviction with the old green. The last time this man picked a loser in an athletic event, college weeklies were carrying liquor adds. If, within ten years the party under consideration does not control the destinies of an entire community by a mere wave of the hand, then we will cut our shoes into small fragments, eat them, and digest them thoroughly. Success, you know, will seek out a man of his ingenuity and magnetic personality.

name: O'NEAL WASHINGTON CHANDLER, B.S.C.
motto: "To will to do is half the deed."
enrolltime: entered the University three years ago
description: "Washington" entered the University three years ago, being one of the boys able to accomplish "4 in 3." During his sojourn with us he has made quite a record for himself as a student and also as an all round college man. He is possessed of a genial, pleasing personality which has won for him many friends. "O. W." is said to be a member of the famous woman haters club, but we notice he is always first to meet the postman and get the little pink missive. He has been an active student in the Commerce Department, having taken a very active part in getting a chapter of Delta Sigma Pi established at the University. At present he confines his ups and downs to Dougherty Street. However, we predict that some day he will ride the ups and downs of Wall Street. Best wishes, "O. W." We know you will make your ups exceed your downs, and that you will do your Alma Mater honor.

name: VIRGIL CARLISLE CHILDS, B.S.A.
motto: "Knowledge and virtue are endowments greater than nobleness and riches."
description: "V. C." came to Georgia in 1916 from the City of Macon. He spent one year in diligent study and then left college to answer the call of his country. After two years' service overseas he returned to the University in 1919 to complete his education. Since then he has shown by conscientious, faithful, and untiring effort that his ambition runs high in the Agricultural world. During his sojourn he has had a wide and varied career. Having learned the fundamentals of stenography in high school, he has made quite extensive use of it in college. In fact, he has made quite an impression upon Dr. Fain as to his ability as an economist. But Farm Management is not the only thing in which "V. C." is interested. He is quite a ladies' man. He bids fair to graduate cum laude. Here's to you, "V. C.," for you have all the attributes of a true gentleman, an excellent scholar, and an admirable companion.

name: CHRISTOPHER THORNWELL CONYERS
motto: "The man that hath no music in his soul is fit for treason, strategy and spoils."
description: Members of the Conyers family have been at the University for the past century, but few, if any, have left the enviable record that "C. T." has. "C. T." is in every sense of the word a young collegian. He is known far and wide, not only for his singing ability but as a ladies' man. "C. T." knows more girls and has more dates than any other fellow in school, but this doesn't keep him from being rather studious. You can readily see from the honors which "C. T." has, that he has made for himself a name long to be remembered. "C. T." grew tired of Georgia and decided to try George Washington for a year, but he grew still more tired of George Washington during his sojourn there, and the following September found him again mingling with his many friends back on the campus. To know him is to like him. May success be yours, Christopher Thornwell!

name: HAROLD MORRIS COOK
motto: "Life is a comedy to those who think; a tragedy to those who feel."
description: Harold, besides being a good all round student, is a veritable sponge of history. Anything that pertains to the records of world affairs or the works of men is his special field. He has a faculty for historical absorption, and the three years that he has spent here have developed him remarkably in the knowledge of the history of the United States and of Europe. A good companion! You could not find a better were you to search for days, Converse! If he has a fault, it is that he neglects his work for conversation. He is a worker, with no regard for Harold Cook, but with a determination to master. He is ambitious to acquire a doctorate and teach history to the youth of America; and if he shows the spirit as a graduate that he has exhibited here, he will succeed in a big way.

name: Guy Harris Cooper
motto: "Let justice be done in every instance."
description: "Guy" is one of the original Columbus Guards of the class of '22. He has not played such an important part in the political broil as some of his fellow-townsmen, but nevertheless, in his quiet way he has made himself felt in his department in the Student Body. He has been a faithful follower of "Little Charlie" and from all reports should bring honor to his profession. He ranks well up in his class and has been honored with membership in the Sine and Tangent, the honorary club among the engineers. His real standing with his associates was clearly established last spring when they asked him to be their representative on the Student Council. This trust he has faithfully fulfilled to the satisfaction of all parties concerned. We know that it will rend his heart to part with "Little" Bailey, so we offer the suggestion that they go into business together. We believe that Guy will reflect credit on his Alma Mater, and we believe that he will succeed.

name: Elisha Carson Cox
motto: "Listen much, but talk little."
description: "Shorty" is our military genius, having risen to the rank of Major, which position he holds down remarkably well. He says one doesn't have to be a "great big" man to have some knowledge of military matters for after all, "wasn't Napoleon of small stature"? Many are the times that we have heard his voice ring out on the drill field, "Where is my d—d Adjutant?" And we knew he meant business, meant for every man in his battalion to be always where he was supposed to be. "Shorty's" entire disposition may well be summed up in his philosophy of life, "Listen much, but talk little," which, together with his other admirable qualities, makes him a great favorite among the students. He is not a mere "babbler" of idle words, but when he talks he speaks volumes, showing that his is a rare instance of a man's choosing a philosophy which applies to himself.

name: JOHN THOMAS COX, JR.
motto: "To be my best self for the general good; to find happiness through sacrifice—my life's philosophy."
description: "J. T." hails from the City of Macon. After he had been graduated from Lanier High School he yearned for higher education, and consequently entered the University. He entered as an humble Freshman and has risen to the rank of a dignified Senior. "J. T." has taken an active part in the work of the Y. M. C. A. and is a leading member of the Student Volunteer Band. The latter organization has led him to take several important conference trips over the state, particularly to G. N. I. C. "J. T." is a hard worker and makes progress in any line he undertakes. His greatest happiness is working for the welfare of others. Luck to you, "J. T." May you find happiness in a life full of good deeds.

name: ARTHUR BENJAMIN CULBERTSON
motto: "The world is calling for real sports—Answer with action."
enrolltime: came to the University three years ago
description: This auburn topped young gentleman came to the University three years ago with a trunk and a determination to carry away a store of knowledge from the Alma Mater of Little Alex and Henry Grady. The ups and downs and the outs and ins that he had in his push to make his way here make the Struggle seem like a club of the sons of the Four Hundred. "Cub" has almost literally lifted himself by his boot straps. Not until his last year in college has he had the time to show what he could do in the life of the University. Debating and public speaking may not be his forts, but he has made his mark in both by passing the early morning hours in penetrative study and arduous practice. Whether it be law or the teaching profession, he will be satisfied with the best.

name: WILLIAM FRANK DANIEL, A. B.
motto: "Work is a remedy for most evils."
clubs: Editor Humorous Issues of the Cracker, '22
description: "Doc" Daniel comes in for his share of the discussion at this time. We want to say right here that the subject is really worth discussing. Frank has been here for four years. Being of a retiring nature he was not known very well during his freshman and sophomore years. However, when the Georgia Cracker first made its appearance he did some excellent work in this notable publication which pushed him to the front among his fellow students. Frank is a modest fellow, preferring to do his best and allow the other fellow to tell the world about it. He never has taken any part in the activities of college life outside of the regular line of duty. He is really appreciated by those that know him best because of his sterling qualities of manliness. Wherever his future life may be spent, we know that the section in which he settles will be the better for his being there. Good luck, Frank!

name: FRANCIS CLEMENTS DART, A.B.
motto: "Nature ever yields reward to him who seeks, and loves her best."
description: Now, fellows, we have with us today a man of many titles and more accomplishments. Call him "Cue Ball," "Pops" or what you may, he came to Georgia in the irresponsible hours of youth, and, having grown up with the country, he now hies himself back to the wiregrass plains of Douglas, where he must eventually develop into a judicial luminary. He will argue anything provided he can find someone to argue with him. He is a good cook, a fine housekeeper, a gifted orator, a close student, a woman hater, and a prince of a fellow. Ladies, it will pay you to become acquainted with the smiling gentleman—he'll make you a mighty fine wife. Wherever he displays the smile, he wins a friend. And "Pop" always smiles. When he frowns all you have to say is, "S'matter, 'Pop'? We're for you, 'Pop.' You're made of regular stuff and you're going to arrive big."

name: ROBERT LEE DASHER, B.S.C.
motto: "What I aspired to be and was not, comforts me."
clubs: Business Manager Glee and Mandolin Club, '22
description: One of the outstanding incidents of a trip south on the "Florida Special" is to see the night capped heads of some of the best Valdostan families peering out as the "cars" go rushing through the birthplace of Robert Lee Dasher. During his sojourn at the University, "Bob" has proved himself a true friend and a loyal student. Never making a fuss or pushing himself forward he has gone steadily on, doing his duties and taking his honors as they came. His regal personality has won him many staunch friends and admirers. We all cling to that code of friendship that regards a friend as a brother, and this nature is exemplified in "Bob." His ability to grasp the situation and probe unerringly to the heart of things, coupled with his steady purpose and his capacity for work, answers his success in whatever he may enter, and it is success that he merits.

name: ALVA BETHEL DAVIS, D.V.M.
motto: "The past is history, the present being good enough, let the future take care of itself."
description: Four years ago this swamp mullet from the southwestern corner of this state came to Georgia along with others from adjoining counties. Davis has been a quiet sort of fellow since becoming a student at this institution. He has not asked for any political honors, and in the Veterinary College they do not recognize such an animal as scholarship. Exposing himself to such a difficult course as that of Veterinary Medicine, he has naturally had to betake himself more to his studies than would have been the case had he been taking a different line of work. But in spite of all the boning "Little Quack" is quite well known. Especially is this true over in New College since the occupants of this venerable edifice were much angered at their being made a target when "Little Quack" takes his weekly Sunday afternoon target practice. We believe that Davis will make a good Veterinarian and will do much to help put the State of Georgia on the map as the producer of livestock.

name: JARRETT LA FAYETTE DAVIS, JR.
motto: "It is easier to be wise for others than ourselves."
description: In September, 1919, a quiet spoken youth jumped off of a Central of Georgia freight train and made a bee-line for Mr. Tom Reid's registration booth. Strangely enough, the boy "quiet-spoke" himself into becoming a student of our University of Georgia, and now we find him quiet speaking his way away from here with a college degree. Oh, Lord Bull, what vagaries are committed in thy name! In his career of quiet-speaking we have found him identified in every phase of college activity. His social proclivities have gained for him the presidency of that social band, the Senate, and his success at quiet-speaking out in the great social field, or parlors, has made him the uncrowned king of any number of feminine hearts. In defiance of any attempt to brain us for using such a harried term, we are knighting him with the classification "a prince of a fellow." Just as long as true ability is recognized, and figures continue not to lie, our man Davis will go along the road of success. The best of luck to a straightforward man's man!

name: JOHN EUSTACE DENMARK
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road and be a friend to man."
clubs: Captain and Regimental Intelligence Officer Cadet Corps
description: Here we are considering the record of "Useless" as some of his more intimate friends speak of him behind his back. Denmark is a jack of all trades, and, to bear out this assertion, we need only point out his record in the various college activities. He has excelled in scholarship, debating, military activities, and last, but not least, in politics. It is probably in the last mentioned phase that he has had the most narrow escapes, winning one election by the margin of one vote while the second resulted in a tie which was broken in his favor. Denmark has played the game fair and square, and his record in this situation stands without blemish. We state without hesitancy that, as an alumnus, he will reflect credit upon his Alma Mater, because he has the qualities which make for success.

name: Ellis Howard Dixon
motto: "An ounce of common sense is worth a pound of book learning."
clubs: Winner Essay on "Great Seal of Georgia"
description: Ellis Dixon, excellent student, writer of winning essays, debater, and a believer in simple honesty. The reputation this young gentleman has made during his three years in the University is one that a man may well aspire to acquire. How has he done it? By unaffected hard work, with a search for his limitations and his faults. He had a good prep record behind him, but past performances played no great part in his work here. He has labored and labored hard. He has been a sincere follower of absolute truth to the best that the Creator breathed into his soul. He is a human being with a realization that men are books, as well as pages bound in cloth or in leather. And he will make good whether his work is known beyond his own door or his name a household word on five continents.

name: Henry Dorman
motto: "A man can do a lot of good if he does not care who gets the credit for it; work and be patient."
enrolltime: finished his course in three years
description: Who is it that doesn't like Henry? He is a hard worker, a good student, writer, soldier, leader, lover, and a gentleman of the very highest type. He has a splendid personality, is a good mixer, a fine fellow, and a true friend. He served his country in France from 1918-1919, and then came back to the University. Here he has not only worked his way through college but he has finished his course in three years. He has taken a leading part in the religious and social activities both on the campus and in the city. Power to you, Henry. The world needs such men as you. May your life be a blessing to humanity.

name: DESSIE BROWN DURDEN, B.S.A.
motto: "True happiness consists, not in a multitude of friends, but in the worth and choice."
clubs: S. A. T. C. Naval unit
description: "Smith" arrived at Georgia in eventful 1918, along with a host of his fellows, had no illusions as to duty--down-right landlubbers from every jungle of the State--his was Emanuel--they sought enlistment in the saltiest branch of the U. S. Navy, the S. A. T. C. Naval unit. He has not yet seen the sea. An ex-salt seldom makes a good land lubber, but "Smith," after four years of devoted worship at the feet of his goddess, the modern science of Agriculture, goes back to the soil of Emanuel to work his miracles in material form. And, brother, if he succeeds in his efforts to make two things grow where one grew before, his college life will not have been in vain. "Dessie" is an adept in the art of cultivation--few men have cultivated more friends in college than he, for he numbers them by his acquaintances. Another angle of his cultivating tendencies explains his hobby--the cultivation of the ladies.

name: ROWIE VINSON DURDEN, B.S.A.
motto: "May I never speak except I have something to say worth while listening to."
description: Again, gentlemen, we have the name Durden; and with it the pursuit of agriculture and the geography of Graymont is synonymously associated. Yet, "R. V." differs from the rest of the Graymontians in that he's the first woman hater ever known to have hailed from their town. Ah, but therein lies a story and a tragedy--for Dan Cupid mixed his fickle arrows and did "R. V." a downright dirty trick. Too much in love to take his college career seriously the first two years, the change into an anti-feminist could not work miracles either. For how can a man study when his heart is filled with unrequited love? But in spite of Fate's hard buffetings, "R. V." has forged ahead and made a record which bears testimony that Cupid can put a man down but never out. He is the only man at Georgia who has lived on every street and every campus during his stay, and as he has numbered his habitations, so has he multiplied his already large circles of friends. Luck to you, "R. V."--the art of agriculture never awaited a more promising evangel of newer and more prosperous seasons.

name: Wilmer Coleman Durden
motto: "And I learned about women from her."
description: A would-be agriculturist—a man who has been training himself for four years to the end that he may don the overalls and chew the regulation straw of a farmer? Like Bill Nye, he has. Somehow we can't visualize him doing either of the above stunts. No, "Goat," we'd picture you far better as a banker or business man. Now, ladies, we wish to introduce a Mr. Farmer Man, who doesn't drive a Fordson or chew Climax. He's a champion ladies man—look him over! In love each week and out the next—we're wondering who the next victim will be. Few farmers can sip the social tea without making a noise like a grain harvester, but, thank God, here's one who's up to Hoyle. "Goat" is a peach of a good fellow, a hard student, in spite of his longing proclivities, and has a veritable multitude of friends who wish him well in the future. There are few men who are as well known over the state as "Goat" Durden. We think we'd capitalize this asset—now that the women vote—and enter politics, old shipmate!

name: Walter Dawson Durden
motto: "Were it not for women, we men might live like gods."
clubs: Member of Student's Loan Fund Board, '20, '21
description: Behold the man! and then we would have you glance casually at the handsome list of honors he has topped. These help to bear out all rumors that he is a student among students and that his four years in College have been profitably spent. "Chubby" is following in the footsteps of a brother who left just such an enviable record as he is leaving, and we are inclined to believe that the scholarly brother has been out-distanced by his younger "Bud." The University is proud of you, "Chubby," and it should be, for you have acquitted yourself well in almost every branch of its activity.

name: Troy Edwards
motto: "He who lives well is a good preacher."
description: This ruddy faced gentleman, a student and an orator by nature; "Beau Brummel" and ardent lover by adaptation, received his first breath of "Georgia" atmosphere in 1917. He came into our community from the 5th District A. & M. School determined to find a better way of tilling the soil and to develop his natural ability as an orator. After four years of diligent application, his success has been so marked that we would unhesitatingly support him for the legislature! However, we doubt if he would care to remain long in politics, for after he has successfully terminated his conquest at the Co-ed Barn, we expect him to settle down and make some North Georgia plantation a mighty good owner. His record is an enviable one and I know we will hear from him in the future.

name: George Reese Evans
motto: "It matters not if I won the game, but it matters greatly how I won it."
description: Back in 1918 before co-education became an established institution, George Reese Evans blew into the Seaboard depot ready to undergo all the trials and tribulations that accompany a freshman's career at our dear University. A likely looking lad, was this Evans person. And until this day, as he grabs his degree, he has retained the likely countenance. But withal he is a confirmed mysoginist. George has fostered, at different times, as many hobbies as we have toes and blistered finger tips. One time cartoonist extraordinary, again a track man of short lived, though decided success, and lastly aid-de-camp to Lional Strongfort, the marvelous physique. In the man's face is honor and loftiness of ideals portrayed in a vividness that defies all description of tongue or pen. The world had best prepare to receive a genius, for determination to succeed in the vast outside is here lacking not. George, we'll back any horse you ride.

name: DOC GEORGE FAULKNER, B.S.A.
motto: "'Bull,' long may it reign, and ever be concealed."
clubs: Secretary-Treasurer and Vice-President of Agricultural Club
description: At last we come to the financial genius of the University. "Doc" is an energetic young (?) fellow who knows the many wiles of the filthy medium of exchange in this noble land of prohibition and I. W. W. He has been very successful because the word "fail" was cut out of his vocabulary many moons ago. "Doc" deserves a lot of credit for his record since entering college; it is one that inspires admiration. He had the earmarks of a successful politician but "Doc" would not let pleasure come before business and as a result he did not enter the slimy political arena. He has budded out as something of a monsoon on the social slop bucket, however, and there are those of us who believe that this is the real reason why he stayed out of politics because he could not spare the time from his social campaigns. There is little use to predict success for "Doc" because he has success by the handle and a down hill pull.

name: RALPH LAMAR FITTS, B.S.
motto: "Care more for the service you sow than for the dollars you reap."
description: Fitts earned the sonorous soubriquet that has attached itself to him in the University. Awkward and with no natural tendency toward the track, he has made an enviable record on the path through sheer determination and hard work. A multitude of duties devolving upon him, he emerges from four years in college with a testimonial of faithful discharge of academic requirements. He is a worker with a clear vision of the things worth while in life. He is planning to follow his father in the study of medicine, and then hie to the mission field and a life of service. Orator, soldier, friend, and a gentleman on and off the track—that is Ralph Fitts.

name: FRANK HAROLD FROST, B.S.C.
motto: "My aim to help the world, as a whole, towards a better way of thinking and living."
description: This is one "Jack" Frost that we are always glad to meet. He came to Georgia in the spring of '20, and has been with us since that time. "Jack" drove up unheralded, without an advance notice of his greatness. Consequently, since he has shown the goods he is truly appreciated by the entire student body. He has worked and worked hard for the glory of his Alma Mater and to him credit is due for many of her victories on the diamond. "Jack" has had no press agent to give him the publicity which his deeds really merit but he has made a name for himself in the memories of his fellow students which will live a good many years. He is a quiet fellow and has not even a touch of conceit about his person. Everybody likes him and this is saying enough for any man. As you go out to fight the battles of life, "Jack," you have the best wishes of us all.

name: FRANCIS MARION GREEN, B.S.C.
motto: "Be brief, and to the point, for brevity is the spice of life."
description: Here is a man who can pull the wool over the eyes of the smartest Prof. In fact, he is reported to have quite a bootlick with Professor Jenkins of the Commerce Department. Judging from outward appearances he looks a great deal like an ordinary person, but take a look at his grades in some nine or ten subjects which he carries in one year and you see that if not extraordinary, certainly he is not mediocre. How he accomplishes so much with so little work is a source of wonder to his classmates, but if one should happen to go to his room in the wee hours of morning one would discover his secret, for he "bones" while others sleep. Frank enjoys college life and seems never to have a worry, nor need he worry about his future, for his sterling work in his classes has marked him a man of rare ability. Dealers in high finance, here is a good partner for you.

name: LUTHER ALFRED GRIFFIN, B.S.A.
motto: "Do what you think is right regardless of what others think, and you will win the respect of them all."
clubs: Twenty-Five Dollar Prize Given by Virginia-Carolina Chemical Company for Essay on "The Profitable Use of Fertilizers with Staple Crops, '19, '20
description: "Griff" is one of the quietest and most seclusive of all the members of the senior class. He has never entered into those college activities which tend to make one well-known. But "Griff" has been a hard worker and stands today as true a Georgia man as breathes. He has one of the best scholastic records of the senior class in Agriculture and as a proper and just reward was made a member of Alpha Zeta. It is a pleasure to know such men as "Griff," because they are constant quantities and you can depend that they speak seldom but truthfully. We understand that he will teach for a while after completing his work here, and we know that wherever he goes Georgia will have an alumnus that she will be proud to acknowledge as her own.

name: HUGH BENJAMIN GURLEY, B.S.C.
motto: "Courage is one of the greatest assurances of a successful life."
description: Although taking Commerce and hailing from Loganville, the two greatest handicaps which could be placed on a Freshman entering this grand old University, "Jim" has shown himself "Fit to Fight," and has surmounted these and other obstacles that have been thrown in his pathway and now finds himself ready to fly away from the top rung of the ladder of collegiate attainment to make his way in the world of business. To those "gentle readers" who do not understand the significance of the above used term of "Loganville," I might add that this is a microscopic town situated somewhere in the wilderness between Athens and Atlanta and famed far and wide for its proximity to Rosebud, the old home town of "Professor" Upshaw. Hugh's friendly attitude and genial disposition have won for him countless friends and admirers, among whom any man in college would be proud to find his name. The business world holds a great future for this young genius of commerce, and when he does get started, look out, Wall Street.

name: EDWARD McLAIN GURR, B.S.C.
motto: "I do all that may become a man; who does more is none."
description: E. McLain Gurr is the cognomen of the Apollo-like member of the genus homo whose classic features are here reproduced. With his name parted in the middle, you failed to recognize the renowned "Ed." Since "Ed" is known where e'er he goes, one might think that he is "sorta" cosmopolitan, but since we have the dope, it is only fair that we explain. His check comes from Macon and he prepped at Lanier High. Our friend "Ed" is quite an athlete, and besides playing a good hand at bridge, he was for two years on the varsity basketball team, playing a forward. Rumor says that basketball is not the only thing he is forward in, and although he is not exactly a woman-killer, all he doesn't kill, he cripples. "Ed" is a hard worker, shown by his graduation in three years. The best wishes of scores of friends go out with him in the struggle of life.

name: JOSEPH HARRY HALPERT, A.B.
motto: "He works and he strives to accomplish an end."
description: Harry Halbert, Sociology fiend, conversationalist, connoisseur of poetry! Harry has succeeded in running the gamut of the four year course in three, and during his stay in the University has established for himself a reputation for doing things in a clear cut fashion. One cannot mistake the diminutive yet business-like figure as it strolls hurriedly across the campus. He has clung close to Peabody Hall, worked busily, talked fluently, and garnered choice bits of sentimental verse. His studies in Sociology have left him in a contemplative mood, and were he not looking toward the medical profession for a career of service, he might spend his time in exhorting the youth of Georgia to prepare themselves for nobler lives. Harry has no marked peculiarities, except the ability to see far into the future of the realistic and idealistic world. He is an excellent student, an idealist, a hard worker, exemplifying a type of manhood that our Alma Mater is always proud to call her own.

name: MARION LOTHROP HANAHAN, B.S.
motto: "For what you believe to be right, fight to the very end."
description: This young man is not a Georgia Cracker, but hails from the State of Alabama. He entered the sacred precincts of the University three years ago. He has worked hard and as a result has succeeded in finishing the general science course in that time. "Lefty" is a quiet fellow and one that will not attract undue notice. But at the same time he is a hard worker and sticks to his task until it is creditably done. He has not shirked the harder courses in order that his scholastic record may reach a dizzy height, but, on the other hand, has seemed to seek those courses, which, while difficult, would do him the greatest good. Hanahan is conservative in his daily life, and we have no doubt but that his is the wiser path. If the past is any sort of a guide to the future, we have no fear for our friend and classmate, "Lefty" Hanahan.

name: ARCHIE DENNIS HARKINS, B.S.A.
motto: "Get what you want, what you can't get don't want."
description: During the fall of '20, this splinter of humanity made his first appearance on the campus of this institution. He immediately became a disciple of the notorious Milton and longed to become as apt a student of the "bull" as his professor. Whether he has attained this standard of perfection is difficult to decide, but it may be said with all truthfulness that he has made great strides in the right direction. "Hawkeye" is a good fellow, liked by all that know him. He is of a jolly turn and always takes a joke as well as he hands one out. That "Hawkeye" takes an interest in the work of his department is evidenced by the fact that he made the Stock Judging team last fall and the part he plays in the Saddle and Sirloin Club. He has made us a good student and we believe that his future work will reflect credit upon his Alma Mater.

name: LUTHER MARION HARMAN, B.S.A.
motto: "To be or not to be."
clubs: First Lieutenant and Adjutant Cadet Corps, '21, '22
description: Another one of the Carroll County boys is up for our consideration. And well may Carroll be proud of her son! "Luke" has made a good all-round record while at the University. He has excelled in scholarship and military activities more, perhaps, than in any other lines. In scholarship his record placed him as a member of Alpha Zeta, while his excellence in the Military Department caused his election to the Scabbard and Blade. He has been a good fellow and has made many friends. Another department in which "Luke" excelled is that of fooling the women. At this kind of work he is an adept. However, we understand that he has not yet attached himself to any girl's apron strings. "Luke" should make a name in after life if he keeps up the stride he has set for himself in college. Shoot, "Luke," or give up the gun!

name: RALPH WALDO HARRIS, B.S.A.
motto: "First, be sure of yourself, then let nothing discourage you from your purpose."
description: "Runt" entered the University four years ago and from the first night until the last night of graduation he could be found in the apartments of Candler Hall. It seems as if "Runt" has become a fixture over in Candler Hall, and that they could hardly do without him when the "bull sessions" start on the steps of that sacred and honored building. "Runt" is a man of few words but when he speaks he has said something. He has a disposition that everybody likes and numbers his friends by those who know him. The only thing that can be said against "Runt" is that it has been rumored that he has roomed with "Skinny" Rivers. But he has quieted this by emphatically denying the charge and offering a handsome reward for its instigator. "Runt" is not the largest man in the class physically, but he makes up for that in the qualities that go to make up a practical student. Luck to you, "Runt." We know that you will do well.

name: DONALD MADISON HASTINGS, B.S.A.
motto: "In what you think; in what you say; in what you do; be a MAN, my son."
description: "Don" has made an enviable record since entering the University. That he can be classed as one of those natural sharks is shown by the list of honors under his name. He is an industrious and conscientious worker, who always puts his whole heart in everything that he attempts to do. "Don" had the good fortune of making the trip with the football team to Harvard as manager last year. There is no doubt as to his future, and we know when he goes back to Atlanta to help his father in the seed business, he will make an even greater success than he has made in college. Georgia will be proud to list you as an alumnus and we know that you will add honor to her name.

name: BOYD DAVID HENDERSON, A.B.
motto: "I neither have nor desire anything."
description: Here he is, the greatest of globe trotters since the crafty Odysseus, who "wandered far and wide and saw the towns of many men." And he says his rambling has only begun and that ere many moons have come and gone, he will have seen the towns of many more men. Be that as it may, "B. D.," we know from experience that you're a good "pal of the road" and number your friends by your acquaintances, so luck to you now and ever afterwards. But despite "B. D.'s" appreciation of "variety in scenery" he has tarried long enough to get a College education and also has cultivated a great habit of reading, with the result that he now stands on familiar terms with the world's greatest authors. So go after it, and, if you don't find it in one place, do as you have in the past, seek it in another. Just remember that the best wishes of your great host of friends go with you in all your undertakings. Let us hear from you.

name: LEWIS HAMILTON HILL, JR.
motto: "Keep on kicking until the hearse comes around."
description: "Flat," gentlemen, is his erstwhile nickname. Whether this refers to his head or his feet, nobody seems to know. Possibly it is because he hails from one of the flat spots around Atlanta, known as Newnan, but more probably it is from the flat sound his feet make as he dashes around in the wake of some young person in skirts. L. H. is a well-rounded man. During his sojourn of three short years he has distinguished himself in several lines. He has made two degrees, A.B. and B.J., as a mark of his scholastic ability. In the line of oratory he has achieved success, both in winning speaking honors and in lending his ability to the direction of Phi Kappa as a member of the Debating Council. To top this record, he is the proud wearer of the "G." "Flat" numbers his friends by scores. We feel that he is going to carry his college success into the battle of life. May his efforts soon be crowned with their just reward.

name: HUGH WILLIAM HOSCH
motto: "Be yourself; the path of simplicity is the best way."
description: "Be yourself; the path of simplicity is the best way." Sage words coming from sage lips, and typified in Mr. Hosch's living. If you want to "fix" a political deal, or attempt to trade a sorry horse, don't go to Hugh. A lion in deliberation, a lamb in personal dealings, as firm as the mountain base, as persistent as the ocean waves that come ceaselessly to the jagged cliffs. These and other descriptions, and you haven't got him half told. The exhibit hails from Gainesville, where all good Georgia men come from, and, by heavens, where they all go back sooner or later, there to become the bulwark of the mountain city. Our man is a connoisseur among the fair sex, and can by a spoken word overcome all their whims and fancies. This ability can be attributed to the proximity of his home to Brenau, where he is known as the fairest among the fair, the Apollo of Apollos. Accept our best wishes for every success.

name: Hurt, Edmund Woodruff
motto: "I am sick of time and desire to rest."
clubs: University of Georgia's Glee and Mandolin Club, '21
description: "Simp" came to the University three years ago from Atlanta, a fact which he has done his best to live down during his stay in Athens. "Simp" can sit and bull for hours at a time, and according to the results obtained in various parts of the State his line must be pleasing. He made the Glee Club in '21, and while under the direction of one of Athens' most noted amateur dancers, developed into the best favorite of the "Harem" that has has been seen in Athens. Among his other attainments while at Georgia, "Ed" made the "Who's Who" of the University as being the most practical man in school. "Simp" has one of the biggest hearts that a student ever had. There never is a time that he cannot help a friend. Luck to you in your life's work, and here's hoping that you go to the very top of whatever profession you choose.

name: Hutcheson, Ware
motto: "Be courteous, have self-confidence and cast the 'ole' bouquet of 'bull.'"
description: "Gravey Hutch" entered in the fall of '18 when Captain Thweat was "Kaiser" of the campus. He was such a quiet sort of fellow that only a few students knew him when he made the baseball team in the spring of '20. Since that time he has been in the limelight, more or less, and now everybody knows him. "Hutch" was not a regular on his prep school nine but since coming here he has worked hard, and, as a result, has developed into one of the best infielders we have. He dislikes too much publicity and praise, but he is worthy of a great deal of it. "Hutch" has made Georgia a good student. By his athletic prowess he has already brought her honor. In the years to come he will win the battles of life if he continues fighting them as he has fought during his four years at Georgia.

name: RUDOLPH JOE INMAN, B.S.C.
motto: "It is not the winning, but how you play, that counts in the game of Life."
description: Boundless energy and enthusiasm for all campus activities, and thorough scholarship in all courses which he has taken up, have characterized the resident of this genial representative of "Old" Florida in our midst. A goodly amount of the sunshine of Florida went into the personal equipment of "Rudy." His infectious smile and winsome ways have won for him a place in the hearts of all who know him, and they are many. Although "Rudy" never had the opportunity of representing the University on a varsity team, those of us who have seen him on the athletic field believe that this is due not so much to lack of ability as to hard luck. His capacity for making friends can scarcely be rivaled by any one in the class, and in the years to come he will surely derive much satisfaction and pleasure in knowing that his personality has won for him so many people to hold him dear.

name: COLUMBUS ANGUS JOHNSON, B.S.A.
motto: "Results to be obtained are more dependent upon methods of procedure than upon efforts exerted in doing."
description: Now, gentlemen, with your indulgence, we will endeavor to do justice to a man who can out parleyvous even the famous J. Lustrat. Where did he learn it? Over across the pond doing his bit to put the war on ice. After experiencing army life, "Frenchy" decided that he could possibly live through at least one college year. He therefore entered the University and found himself rooming in Candler Hall. "Frenchy" completed the Candler Hall and Freshman courses all in one year, which is some task in itself, and then spurted up to make the next three laps in two, which he has done. He has made Georgia an excellent student and has the unique record of being the only man who could nightwatch all night and attend class all day. It is generally thought that he will endeavor to pass on to the coming generation of towheads some of the information he has gathered together in the last three years. If so, we predict that he will make them an excellent teacher.

name: Dessie Ford Johnson
motto: "The man who serves his neighbor can not but lead a broad life."
description: "Dess" came to the University three years ago with a large store of pep and determination. His record as a Freshman was in keeping with fair scholarship, but in his second year he began to show his real worth. Carlyle and Arnold fell before his onslaught and the Economics Department held no terrors for him. He read a goodly number of the classics, thought a great deal, and came back to summer school determined to carry away the testimonial of the faculty in three years. Besides driving through the summer academic work, he daily recreated his weary spirit by comforting homesick school marms. No accurate record of his activities on the campus is available, but no one ever has disputed his claim of supremacy in pursuit of the "campus course." He is a worker with the vision of the possibilities that lie before the college man. He has done things here; he can succeed anywhere. May the best of luck be yours, "Dess."

name: Thomas Giles Kelly
motto: "Nothing except life itself is so interesting as life as it is imagined."
description: Why do they call him "Boozy"? Nobody knows why anyone would wish such a name on such a good fellow, and especially in this day and time, where it flows not at all. But then let us admit that he has been done a great injustice. "Boozy" is one who deserves the appellation, "A gentleman and a scholar." Although unassuming and reserved, his social graces, his sterling qualities of character and his fine personality have won for him the affection and honor of the entire student body. Books have never bothered him, but he is conscientious and uses his time to an advantage, although he will take an hour off every now and then and join "Morpheus" in a slumber party. With all the pride of a true southerner, he is typical of the south in generosity, hospitality, and true gentlemanly traits. We wish him the happiness and success that he deserves.

name: CLINTON COLEMAN KEMP, B.S.A.
motto: "The Bull is mightier than the Bullet."
description: "Clinton" is a big man with a big heart. He always greets you with a broad hand and a broader smile. The fact that "C. C." hails from Powder Springs, is the only thing we have against him. After repeatedly being lured on by some of his associates, he joined the ranks of the Book-Agents and tried to do missionary work in the Virginia mountains. While in the Old Dominion it is rumored that "Clinton" so captivated the heart of one of the beautiful girls of that State, that he has since been kept busy reading telegrams and special delivery letters. It seems that he is a regular vamp because he has maintained complete sway over the heart of a certain Co-ed, while two of his classmates tried all the wiles in their power to win her hand. But above all, Kemp is a loyal Georgia man. Luck to you, you have our best wishes.

name: JOSEPH TOLLESON KIRBY, JR., A.B.
motto: "The surest way to hit a woman's heart is to take aim kneeling."
enrolltime: During his four years in our midst
description: J. T., Jr., alias "Tolly," "Rabbit," etc. During his four years in our midst he has been a good student, in fact, he stands among the first four hundred in scholarship. The "Rabbit," like most of the species, likes to browse on the green and under the tutelage of "Pop" has become fairly adept in the subject of "Q's." Though a southerner by birth and inclination, for the past two seasons "Tolly's" thoughts during the school months have wandered North of the Mason-Dixon Line. Vacation time finds his habitat in Newnan and Atlanta. We understand that he is commercially inclined, and expects to go to Columbia next year. Whatever his aim may be he has the best wishes of his many friends. Of one with such a youthful face and innocent smile, we would say he has yet lots of time in which to propound such thoughts, but looks are often deceiving, and he may be a regular "Devil" in his own home town.

name: WILLIAM LAWTON LAMB, B.S.C.
motto: "Look for you and yours, and let the other fellow do the same."
description: A keen observer is this young fellow who came to us some four years ago, from Swainsboro. He has developed some admirable tendencies since he landed, and every fellow who knows Lamb is fond of that genuine good disposition that is so peculiarly his own. If any one has ever been offended by this fellow it is not known. His sense of humor is a stimulus, and Lamb has tided himself over the hard places in his career at the University and smiled at the worst of things. There is no beating around the bush with this fellow. When there is danger of intensifying a weakness, Lamb believes in removing the danger rather than waiting for the result and then making an application for the removal of the injury. His career has been modestly carried out here, however. Lamb has been on the alert gathering manna for the future, and we expect to hear much of him in the business world. Boy, good luck!

name: LONNIE RICHARD LANIER, B.S.A.
motto: "Which is the greater, 'a Senior who has never been a Freshman or a Freshman who never expects to be a Senior.'"
description: "Lonnie" started in the University back in 1916, but had to take a vacation in order that he might cross the Atlantic and quiet a certain chap named William, who was, about that time, kicking up a good deal of disturbance. Since returning from the Army, he has debated a little, and taken one or two flings into politics. Because he is a born leader of men, he was made a Captain in the M. T. C. However, the outstanding feature of "Lonnie" is his uncanny ability to vamp all the girls he meets, and keep his own heart intact all the while. But we fear that some letters that he has been getting from a certain South Georgia town will spell defeat, and that before many moons have passed he will have launched his bark on the troubled seas of matrimony. "Lonnie," luck to you, and may all your troubles be little ones.

name: FRANK ALSTON MARTIN, B.S.A.
motto: "For my friends I will go the limit."
description: Well, gentlemen, if any, here is the man who stands up under the name of Frank Alston, and we'll say that the name has as sound a resting place as any we know. Frank has made this University a good student and furthermore, we know that he will make her a better alumnus. This young man has never "put out" for the various medals offered for scholarship, nor has he gone out after the political scalp of some of his fellow students, but he has always proved ready to help his friends in any way possible, and for this one characteristic he has made many true friends. One of the worst things we can say about Frank is that he is frank. In fact, we do not know of anyone whose tongue and brain co-operate so well. If a thought comes to Frank's mind, a moment later it comes off his tongue. Frank is easily soothed to sleep (sweet, restful sleep) by the monotonous monotone of a mossbacked professor, and as a consequence the title of "Sleepy" has been wished off on him. We do not call him "Sleepy" being on too familiar terms with him for that, but at any rate we believe that he was a good baby. Frank is strictly business, and should develop into one of the moneyed aristocracy.

name: JAMES LEWIS MERRITT, B.S.A.
motto: "It is easier to find fault than to be faultless."
description: "J. Lewis" hailed from the small Hamlet of Americus four years ago with the intention of making a real "farmer" of himself. Should he be as successful in climbing the ladder of success in life as he has been in climbing Ag. hill, he will surely do well. During "Jimmie's" sojourn with us he has made a great record for himself as a student. He is a hard working scout, possessed of a modest, friendly disposition, which has won for him many friends. In the field of cattle judging, this man of precision, has no peer. It is rumored of late, that he is especially good on "calves." If he is as good in judging the ladies as in judging stock, we predict for him a happy home. Above all, he is a well rounded student and could do well in any walk of life. Judge well, old boy, and success is yours.

name: HAROLD MERCER MORRIS
motto: "Don't look for wider fields until you have mastered those about you."
description: This young man came to us only three years ago from county of Carroll. He lives up this name marvelously well, for his fine voice has thrilled more than one Athens maiden. Judging from the number of letters he gets now since his Glee Club trip we believe that there are girls in other parts of the state affected in the same manner. "Zeek" has gained a notorious reputation this spring for his ability to tell good jokes. It has even been suggested that a College of Jokes be instituted in the University, with "Zeek" as Dean; also that he be made head of the Division of Co-operation, since his connection with the Co-op during his stay here has eminently fitted him for the position. With these two qualities, joviality and co-operation, we can see nothing for "Zeek" but sunshine and the pot of gold at the foot of the rainbow.

name: OLIVER SAMUEL MORTON
motto: "He who knows much has many cares."
clubs: Second Lieutenant Co. B, R. O. T. C.
description: When it devolves upon us to speak a word concerning this boy, we fall back apace, and are confounded. S'blood! The fellow, it is said, is a veritable night hawk, and lays his magnetic plans after midnight. His nocturnal ramblings have been productive of countless honors, as a glance at his coterie of distinctions will prove. Some wag has said, however, that Sam is one of those Bacchanalian adherents who care not for the length of the road, but are greatly disturbed at times about the width of it. The women have not escaped his charms, either. In requital of his affection, they have concocted for him various terms of endearment, which we, for the sake of harmony in the family, will not divulge. Summing up, we find these qualifications: A master of the literary pen, the shaker of a mean oratorical tongue, and a prince of a fellow.

name: JIM CARSWELL MURPHEY, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Give all thine ear but none thy tongue for silence is a rare gem."
description: "Jim" came to us four years ago from the quiet town of Hephzibah and each year that he has been with us he has bound himself closer to our hearts. To know him is to love him and to be a friend of his is an honor worth striving for. "Jim" does not indulge in idle talk, but when he speaks his words abound in wisdom. This gentleman is the primary cause of the electric company's wealth—as he is a burner of the midnight oil. Dame Rumor has it that somebody is waiting down in Hephzibah for him besides his little brother, and we are inclined to believe her in that respect. If "Jim" works as hard in the future as he has while in the University, his success is a certainty. The very best luck to you for the future, "Jim."

name: WILBUR THOMAS MURRAY, B.S.C.
motto: "Let us regard our mistakes, not as stumbling blocks but as stepping stones to greater success."
description: Looking back to the year 1917, it will be remembered that this boy wonder came to the classic city to help further the social world of the University. During this year, Wilbur lead the Freshman Club through one of the best years in the history of the organization. After finishing the horrors of the S. A. T. C., he decided to explore the golden west. Fully realizing his mistake, he returned to our folds. From this time on, he has worked with all the complicated affairs of the Commerce Department. With only three years to his credit, we would say that his record is one that many envy, having been among the leaders in all of his class work. To this we may add in a summary way, that our friend is one we shall never forget, one that we shall always remember as a clean fighter and a gentleman in the true sense of the word. Success is yours, accept it.

name: JOHN WILLIAM McCRANEY, B.S.C.M.A.
motto: "I would rather be right than President."
description: Now we come to the young man who has managed more athletic teams for Georgia than any other student in her history. This activity of "Bill's" has earned him the title of "Manager." He has been a faithful servant in this capacity and holds the sincere appreciation of those in charge of athletics. But this is not the only line in which "Manager" has excelled, for he holds the Phelps-Stokes Fellowship, and then there is politics, in which he has been more successful in politicking for the other fellow. However, "Manager's" own ambitions fell down because of the failure of certain friends to stand by him. In all the different phases of college life in which "Mack" has participated he has played the game square. If he wins he is a gracious victor, and if he loses he takes loss like a man. With this quality alone, success should reward his efforts.

name: DONALD WALLACE McFARLAND, B.S.C.
motto: "Strive to make an honest living and help others at all times."
description: "Mac" hails from the fair mountains of Northwest Georgia. He entered the University four years ago, with the spirit to make good, and this spirit has predominated in his life through these four years. He has fought a good, clean battle all the way through. There has not been a harder and more conscientious worker here than "Mac" has been. Neither has a man pulled more for "Old" Georgia. When that old team went out to fight, no matter in what line of athletics, to him it was all for Georgia, and "Mac" could always be heard above all others, whether the team was leading by a safe margin, or whether she was in the hole, he always yelled encouragement. That spirit of never say quit ruled with him. "Mac" has done much and said little; on through life he will take the ideal that "Actions speak louder than words." "Mac," we all know that you will make Ole Georgia proud of you.

name: PAUL McGEE, B.S.C.
motto: "Let's live more and quit existing."
enrolltime: During the four years he has taken to finish his course in commerce
description: Languages are Paul's forte, but he is an admirable student in other ways. During the four years he has taken to finish his course in commerce, he has done each subject as if it were the only one required of him. Spanish and French are at his beck and call, and any medium of communication that he attempts to master is almost equally as docile under his power of imagination. Aside from his good record as a student, Paul is a good thinker, a pure thinker, the possessor of a spirit without guile or malice. His disposition is toward the ideal, and he lives the principles that he maintains to be holy. His environment has served to draw from the depths of his soul the best that is in him, and he leaves the University with his face set toward a noble end. His comrades are for him; they believe in him! they feel that he will succeed.

name: THOMAS LEVERETT McMULLIN, B.S.A.
motto: "Think then you are today what yesterday you were—Tomorrow you shall not be less."
enrolltime: In his four years at Georgia
description: Halt!—Stop!—Look!—Listen! This is a man from Hart County. The county where men fall down in the snow and freeze because of being overloaded with joy-oil. But we can truthfully say that never would "Mac" get in such a condition as that. "Mac" served his apprenticeship out in noble service for his country as a member of the Saturday Afternoon Tea Club. After that time he wended his way over on the brow of the Ag. Hill to offer himself for the advancement of scientific agriculture. He is now ready to put into practice the facts he has garnered, or to pass the information on to a younger generation. In his four years at Georgia, "Mac" has made many friends because of his jolly, carefree nature. He is a good sport and will play you fair in all things. The acquaintance with such men as "Mac" is what makes life worth living. As you leave us, old man, you have our best wishes for your success.

name: Malcolm Angus McRainey
motto: "Nothing is certain."
clubs: Freshman, Sophomore and Junior Scholarships in Agriculture
description: Ladies and gentlemen, here he is. "Mack" is, without a doubt, the biggest man in College, physically and otherwise. Elmodel can well be proud of her son, and they can well turn out the town when he goes home, for "Mack" has come here to conquer, and now there are no new worlds to conquer. In his studies, he is one of the most brilliant men in College. In politics he is the idol of his friends and the fear of the rival factions. And in his literary work, this book will speak for itself. "Mack" married before he entered here, and there is a "Little Mack," who will be a political leader here about 1942. Luck to you, "Mack," you ought to make Baker County a good Ordinary.

name: Norman Dunning Nickerson
motto: "Do as you have been 'done' by."
description: "Nick" is a man with driving power and to spare. He goes after things and he usually gets them. His record in the University has been creditable, but he has left the impression with those that know him best, that he has not let himself out as he is sure to do when he begins structural execution. Like a good debater or a good pitcher, he has not let the difficulties that he has met so far have the force of all the power he commands. He is a firm believer in believing in the things that you are doing and doing them with zeal. A man of good judgment, excellent tastes, a friend of the right, and, above all, a gentleman—that is Norman Nickerson.

name: ERNEST MORGAN NIX, B.S.A.
motto: "The deed I intend is great, but what, as yet I know not."
enrolltime: fall of the S. A. T. C. and Flu Epidemic
description: "Nix" is one of the old guard which entered in the fall of the S. A. T. C. and Flu Epidemic. He served his sentence out in that noble organization, and came back to Georgia to get an education. "Nix" has been a consistent, hard worker. He never has tried to cop first honor, but just the same, he has done creditable work in his classes. In other lines he has done even better and holds one of the best debating records any student ever made in the College of Agriculture. His entire record shows "Nix" to have been one of the best men we have had with us in many a day. The feeling which his fellow-students have for him is only the best, for he is liked by all. "Nix" may teach or farm, but at either he will succeed. Luck to you, Ernest.

name: CHRISTOPHER C. PEARCE, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "What should a man do but be merry."
enrolltime: After two years of cadetship at "The Citadel"
description: If there are many more men in the University more widely known and more agreeably known, we miss an official guess. Hailing from the renowned old state mentioned above, "C," as he is better known, directed his first efforts towards a higher education to "The Citadel." After two years of cadetship at "The Citadel," he decided to return to his native state, as he is one of Georgia's own sons, his birth-place being Wigham. "C" has proved an excellent quarterback and there never was a time when he was called on, and the times were many, that he couldn't fill the bill. He was extremely cool, and picked the weak spots with rare precision. "C" was a real quarterback. He possesses the type of manhood that our Alma Mater is always willing to call her own. A true friend, a genial companion, ever willing to leave his path to please another. You can mark him as a man.

name: TERREL RAINEY PERRY, JR., A.B.
motto: "Think only of your work until your task is completed."
description: "Commodore" came tripping in from McPhail Institute (which in plain English means Sylvester High). It is a great transition, coming from the little town of Sylvester, basking sleepily under a South Georgia sun, to promenading up and down Milledge Avenue, in front of Lucy Cobb, but that is what our hero has accomplished. Although his middle name has some degree of dampness about it, he nevertheless spreads sunshine wherever he goes. Not content with having romped away with the necessary sixty-nine hours in a short three years, Perry has decided that he has not yet displayed his ability in full, and next year any one who wishes to lead the Sophomore Law class will have some first class proposition. Perry has a happy smile and a glad hand for his friends, and those who know him are those who like him. With such assets he is certain to achieve success.

name: ABNER THADDEUS PERSONS, B.S.
motto: "Always be true to the best that is in you."
