description: Brilliant! No. Consistent! Yes; and a worker of truth and righteousness. A worker and a gentleman under adverse or in favorable surroundings—that is Abner Thaddeus Persons. A splendid record in prep school, where he made friends by the score, and a bulldog determination have enabled him, financially unaided, to finish in twenty-seven months instead of the thirty-six, the rocky course leading to his degree. Usefulness in whatever sphere he is thrown and the ideals of the great are his natural characteristics. His thoughts run easily along the lines of the works of the literary masters. Of "Thad" it is conservative to say that he will make good in whatever field he chooses to labor. He has earned the respect and the admiration of his comrades, and they are with him strong in his work. Power to you, "Thad."

name: ROBERT CARTER PITTMAN, A.B.
motto: "To make all men my friends, within the law of reciprocity."
description: This sorrel topped prodigy hails from the northwestern extremity of this noble state. While he registers from LaFayette, we personally think that probably what he means is that it is from this village that he hauls his guano. "Red" slipped up on us three years ago and immediately set about making a little history for himself. He never "put out" for the A-plus marks, but he passed his work creditably and found sufficient time to engage in other college activities. He has made speaking honors galore and has successfully dabbled in politics. "Red" thinks that he can sing and he evidently had the Glee Club leader fooled, for he made that Club with the terrible handicap of being a freshman. The campus dope has it that "Red" will spend his life, after he has taken law at some other institution, in the defense of the worthy bootlegger. Go to it, old man, but we feel for that bootlegger.

name: ROBERT HEADDEN POWERS, B.S.C.
motto: "Labor, laughter and love are the measures from which the cup of life is filled."
description: "He was the noblest Roman of them all," and like the great Roman, "he came; he saw; he conquered." This is to inform you few who do not know the distinguished record of this gentleman from Floyd. Coming to us three years ago, a meek freshman, he has during this short time ascended to the ethereal heights of a noted scholar. "Bob" is a hard worker, having spent much time in toiling over various and sundry financial records of the War Memorial Fund. We understand from all reports that Powers is not satisfied with the education received here, extensive as it is, and intends to journey forth to Columbia to augment his present knowledge there. A man devoted to his study, sincere in his purpose, we predict for him no less achievement than his ambition demands. Thus it will be that before long one can read on the frosted-glass door of some office, "Robert H. Powers, C. P. A."

name: JOHN FRANKLIN REID, B.S.A.
motto: "I try to be 'FRANK' with everybody."
enrolltime: entered the S. A. T. C. in 1918
description: Greeting to the gentleman from Bowdon Junction. "Frank" came to the University and entered the S. A. T. C. in 1918; he has since that time displayed his Military ability in the R. O. T. C. in which he is now a first lieutenant. Frank has not failed to do his share in the propagation of Campus traditions, having domiciled at Candler Hall, Lucas Hall and Old College. He has been closely associated with the Y. M. C. A. and the "Beanery"; also he has been an active member of both the Demosthenian and the Agricultural Clubs, from which he will receive diplomas. During his college career, he has been intimately connected with the State Normal, and has gone on record as having no objection to Co-education at Georgia so long as he is so fortunate as to find a playmate for his favorite sport—tennis. His pleasing personality, seriousness of purpose, general ability and fearlessness of work will assure his ultimate success. Say it with five, Good luck to you.

name: LELAND CHADWICK REW, B.S.A.
motto: "It does not pay to worry—things are bound to happen anyway."
description: The first time we saw Rew, we wondered if that smile was natural or simply the result of a futile attempt to fathom the depths of a civilization that possessed such unknown articles as electric lights and other things not found in Forest, Mississippi. Rew has made a good record in his work, especially in the Animal Husbandry Division. It is rumored that Leland intends to cross the horse and kangaroo so that the "buggy horses" of Mississippi will find it easier to jump the ditches along the road. "Leland's" biggest virtue is that he is always willing to help a friend. His genial personality has made friends out of all his acquaintances. We hope that he will stay in Georgia, but if his native state calls him back, we trust that he will find someone that will be true to him and will settle down and help make the world a better place to live in.

name: OWEN REYNOLDS
motto: "A life worth living is certainly one well worth moulding with the utmost care."
description: Little light can be had on the past history of this modern D'Artagnan except that he first saw the light of day somewhere in the wilds of what is now known as Seminole County, prepped at Tifton Ag., and allowed Mr. Tom Reid to add another name to the celebrated Reid mental roster in 1915; then after quitting his books for two years to fly the flag of his country from the tail spin of a seagoing hawk plane, he at last came to earth with his helmet on. Craving excitement of a mild sort, he straightway trotted onto the gridiron field and finally lead his team in the fight. An athlete, a scholar, a soldier, yet a peace-loving man, a forceful, earnest, likeable chap—he is D'Artagnan still—he turns from past adventures to enter the quiet, yet still exciting lane of letters. As captain of his team he fought for all that was manly and fair, never failing to play up and play the game. They are waiting for you somewhere, Owen, to lead again.

name: HENRY ALLEN ROBINSON
motto: "May right be the guiding light of my life."
description: "Henry, having come to us from the Richmond Military Academy, receives a degree from the most difficult course in college after only three years here. This is quite an honor, for anyone exposed to "Little Charlie's" courses does well to master them in four years. But "Henry" has also divided his activities with Dr. Stephens by taking the "Higher Math." He is quite a mathematics shark and can solve any "thirteenth" degree equation known. He has met all the math requirements for the M.S. Degree. "Henry" is quite a busy man but nevertheless he always finds time to slip off to Augusta for the week ends. There certainly must be some attraction down there for him, for he has spent only three week ends in Athens since first coming here. We have been worried for fear he would get "tied up" there and not get back, for he is rather strong for the fair sex. "Henry" has not yet fully decided what he will do next year, but he is seriously considering an "invitation" to attend a school up north, and continue along the mathematical line. Luck to you, "Henry," success is sure to come your way.

name: CHARLIE ASBERRY ROYSTON
motto: "Work hard and success will be yours."
description: After securing all the knowledge which the fair Metropolis of Royston, Ga., had to offer, this young lad rambled over to the University four years ago, in quest of greater learning. He was quite able to show the Freshmen a good time during his sophomore year. As a vamp, Charlie has made a wonderful success, so far as a certain young lady is concerned. At almost any time he may be seen wending his way down College Avenue towards this young lady's residence. "Chollie" was so fascinated with drilling that he has stuck to it for four years. For lack of anything better to do, he entered the Commercial Department and has fared rather well under the guidance of the far famed "Pistol." "Chollie" takes things as they come and never worries about tomorrow. However, he is a typical jolly good fellow and numbers his friends by scores. "Chollie" hasn't yet decided what he is going to do in the future, but we all feel sure that he will bring honor to his Alma Mater and make a brilliant success in after life.

name: MALLON JEROME SHEFFIELD
motto: "Aim high and shoot straight. You can't miss when you're got dead aim."
description: Ladies, this is Mr. Sheffield! However, it might be more fitting to introduce him to the gentlemen, for, if he is unknown to either sex, it is the male. During Mallon's four-year sojourn at the University he has accomplished much in every activity with which he has associated himself. He has been the shining light of the Glee and Mandolin Club since his first appearance, holding every important office in it. It is not our idea, however, to leave the impression that the musical line is the only one in which he has starred. For besides the long line of friends he has made since entering college, he has attained many student honors, and has shown great aptitude in the classrooms. If he tackles the world as hard as he did college—look out, world!

name: Ransom Harris Skeen
motto: "Be yourself above all things."
description: Ransom has been a student at the University for three years, and when we say student we mean it in the true dictionary sense of the word, and not in the college slang idea. He has been a hard and conscientious worker and his work has been crowned with success. Aside from scholastic honors, Ransom has gathered no little fame as a musician. For two years he was a member of the Mandolin Club, and, joined with other dark haired brutes, he toured the State on a heart-breaking expedition. He is always a prominent member of the band until after it makes it's annual trip to Columbus. In spite of studies and music, Ransom has found time to make scores of friends, and his natural capacity for leadership has placed him at the top of everything he undertook. May your success in life increase with your years, old boy.

name: Charles Morse Slack
motto: "My life will carve its own Philosophy and may other men profit by my mistakes."
description: It was in the fall of '18 that this youthful scion of the Slack family entered "Georgia." Since that time he has been more or less devoted to duty and has collected many college honors. Slack is a speaker of ability, but his record in his class work shows that his chief interest lies in his studies. As a Sophomore, he tried, to the limit of his ability, to impress the Freshmen with their utter lack of understanding. His favorite pastime was concerned with the unpadded pants and the oaken board. There are many men in college who remember Slack's night raids with fear and trembling. He has made Georgia an excellent student. We do not know his plans for the future, but we have no fear but that he will continue his present stride, no matter what he undertakes.

name: JOHN ROBERT SLAUGHTER, B.S.A.
motto: "It matters little whether I win, but it matters greatly how I fight."
description: "J. Rich" came driving up in the fall of '18 along with the other members of this class. He had hardly stopped on the campus when he received a bid to "Zeta Chi." Though almost persuaded to accept, he decided to hold off for a few days and then he became wise to the snares set by the upperclassmen. John has been guyed quite a bit because of the numerous high school medals with which he adorned himself during the first few weeks of his existence at "Georgia." As he became more sophisticated the medals disappeared; at the end of his first term he was a regular fellow. The bad luck in his first and second years resulted in his getting behind in some of the required courses, but he has made these up by hard work, and will finish up all right this year. John is not a boisterous fellow, but one tending to be quiet. He is devoted to the B. Y. P. U., especially if there is a large female membership. From his recent activity, we believe that he is to teach. If he does, his training here will insure him success.

name: EMMETT NORMAN SMITH, B.S.C.
motto: "For too much rest itself becomes a pain."
description: Emmett, as he is known by all of his friends, is universally liked. Because of his very nature, he seems incapable of offending anyone. His smile is one that never wears off, and if he has any troubles they are not evident. Emmett has been with us intermittently for five years. During his course, he took a furlough to engage in the scrap with Kaiser Bill. He also cast his lot with the oil men of Louisiana, but he came back, and accordingly gave us the privilege of expecting to point to him some day and say "He was a classmate of mine." He stands ace high with the fair sex, and we feel sure that ere long he will be snowed under by the sweet voice of a certain young girl. Truly, everyone who knows him can best say, this is the man after mine own heart.

name: FREDERICK BUREAN SMITH, B.S.A.
motto: "To reach the height of human endeavor is to be a man."
description: "Big Smith" has a peculiar way of winning friends and keeping them. Not only has he proved himself a desirable fellow to have around, but he has proved himself to be a man in every respect. His life here will be an inspiration to others when they find that he is one of the few who finished the B.S.A. course in three years and at the same time worked his way through. When he is gone it will be said of him, "He fought a good fight, kept the faith and finished his course." "To be a man is the height of human endeavor;" is the motto of his life. He has made an excellent record at the University of Georgia, of which any one should be proud.

name: JOSEPH LECONTE SMITH, A.B.
motto: "To give the world my best and every man his due."
description: "Count" began his college career at Mercer, he being of such active proclivities that it was thought best to keep him in an environment perhaps more sheltered than he would find at the University—especially as his family was living in Macon and could keep an eye on him. Our hero decided that beginning with his junior year he would follow his original plan and risk himself as a student at Georgia. Anybody observing him can recognize that the present "J. L. C.", whatever his scholastic origin, is a mighty good man. He is systematic, industrious, persistent in good works; above all, he is fair-minded, and he has a ready sympathy for all sorts of people, of whom he does not necessarily demand that they see life from just his angle. According to schedule, "Count's" next stop will be at Harvard, where he is to study law. He will do well there, as he will do at any other place at which there is a premium set on real worth.

name: JAMES PAYNE SPICER, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Stand by your friends—right or wrong."
description: During football season "Jimmie" divides his time between "Little Charlie" and Sanford Field. In fact, he likes both so well that he came back to us for an extra year just to delve deeper into the mysteries of Lanza and the three Moment Equation; at the same time shifting his dogs with remarkable celerity on the gridiron. One would not expect to meet a social lion in such a practical being as an engineer, but here we have the unusual combination of Ladies' Delight in a Porch Swing and "Spike" Spicer looking through a Wye-Level. "Jimmie" is one of the most likeable fellows in college. Those who know him are his friends. As the possessor of a good nature and ability, we cannot but predict for him a big success.

name: CHARLES DANIEL STEWART, B.S.A.
motto: "Help yourself rise by helping others rise."
clubs: Secretary of Y. M. C. A., '21, '22
Baritone in the Red and Black Quartet
description: All of us like "Sunshine." He has a very pleasing and attractive personality. Always very popular he is a hard worker and a really fine fellow. He is noted far and wide as a singer. Georgia is going to be proud of this man. The list of honors after his name is an index to his good record. He has taken an active part in the religious and social activities on the campus and in the city. He has filled a big place in the University life. We are sure that he will fill a much bigger place in the life he is about to enter. We are counting on you, "Sunshine." May your life be happy and a benefit to humanity.

name: James Raysor Stokes
motto: "Real happiness comes only through the conscious pursuit of a worthy purpose."
description: From the marshlands and frogponds of South Georgia, out of the sticks and hayseeds of the backwoods, steps a man who is leaving his footprints on the sand of "Georgia's" history, and who gives promise of scratching still deeper in the mould of the intellectual world ere he has shuffled off this mortal coil. Such a diversified character, with so many striking and admirable qualities speaks for itself much more eloquently than all the eulogies penned by hand could do. But let it suffice to say that James can drape his habit over his manly form much more becomingly than Beau Brummel, can grace the Senior Round Table as well as King Arthur's most chivalrous knight, and can get as much out of his books as the best of students. A keener sense of duty, a more resolute determination, a more sparkling intellect, or a larger heart seldom dwells in house of clay.

name: William Lacy Tait
motto: "Know at least one thing well, know much about many things; a well rounded man is well met."
description: "Lacy" entered the University in the fall of '19. He claims Brunswick as his home, a little seaport village in the original "Sandskeeter" country. He was green as the "Marshes of Glynn" in summer, with just enough salt from the sea breeze to save him from being verdant. Never was there a more consistent worker. Aside from doing four years in three, he mixed with the boys enough to be known as a hail-fellow-well-met, and a man of genuine worth. "Lacy" has ignored the ladies while here. We have a sneaky feeling that it is on account of his abhorrence of having someone sit on his lap and rumple his hair. He rarely goes nearer profanity than an emphatic "Gosh-Ding-It." His ready wit and quiet dignity demand the respect he deserves. You must meet this fellow at your earliest convenience. May the best be yours, "Lacy," wherever you go.

name: PRESTON CARL UPSHAW, JR., B.S.C.
motto: "I will let the careers of nobler men be the philosophy of my life."
description: "Willie" believes that "Variety is the spice of life." He hails from the city of Rosebud, the famous suburb of Loganville. When Co-eds first entered the University, he was an exponent of the doctrine of "Total Exclusion," but from causes unknown, he has been converted, sprinkled and baptized in the faith that "woman hath charms to soothe the savage breast." He now dresses so conspicuously that one female student has said, "The Prince of Wales is with us." Seriously, "Willie" is a leader in the School of Commerce. For four years he has displayed his ability and capacity for work, and his readiness for action places him among the minority whose quick mentality creates thoughts which crowd the tongue for expression. He never leaves a task incomplete, but thoroughly cleans the slate before he leaves it. We predict that he will be nothing less than a giant in the world of business and finance.

name: ENOCH JAMES VANN
motto: "I abandon not hope, for it is written that the meek shall inherit the earth."
description: At first glance one would think "Vann" a child of the metropolis Boston, but you must guess again. This Boston is a hamlet within the borders of our own state, and "Vann" is one of its most worthy sons. "Vann" has worked hard every one of the three years that he has been in our midst and if hard work and conscientiousness have anything to do with success, Enoch's achievements will surpass the wildest dream of ambition. It would not be amiss to add right here that "Vann" is another letter hound, he receives more letters in one week than the rest of us receive in six months, and all of his letters bear the same postmark and are addressed in a feminine hand. Enoch has never been known to utter more than ten words in a single day, which is due, however, to something that we have mentioned before. Best of luck to you, Enoch.

name: JAMES MILLER WALKER, JR., B.S.
motto: "He, alone, is courageous who never despairs."
description: And all the maidens shouted, "'Tis he, 'tis, our ideal at last." Gentle reader, scan these few lines with care. The physiognomy here portrayed, of the one and only Miller, can be seen often, in life, roaming around his native haunts in the Queen City of Augusta, Ga. The Academy of Richmond County sent him out, one of its proudest products, and turned him loose without warning on our unsuspecting University. During his three years stay here he has made his B.S. degree, and a brilliant record, to say nothing of many true friends. Though of a somewhat quiet nature, to him an argument on any and all subjects is more welcome than the flowers in May. This young man has a keen and clear mind, with which he can make whatever tasks he chooses to apply it to come out just the way he wishes. He is a true gentleman and a loyal friend. May the best of luck attend you, Miller, and success meet you in whatever path of life you choose.

name: EARLE ERASTUS WATSON, A.B.
motto: "Perseverance and the will to do are the cardinal qualities."
description: This busy man is none other than the Editor-in-Chief of our weekly publication, The Red and Black. He it is who supplied said paper with enough copy to make of it a five column affair, the pride of the University and the envy of College Weeklies in general. Earle is somewhat of an orator and debater, and holds the honor of being "big borer" down in Phi Kappa. Since teasing the "Bulldog" is his hobby, much of his time is spent enacting scenes suitable for publication in this notorious column. He tickles the typewriter for more than one metropolitan newspaper, an activity attested to by the fact that one wall of his den is papered with such clippings. With his determination, Earle is bound to achieve success in the world of journalism.

name: ROBIN MADISON WHEATON, B.S.C.
motto: "May each of us strive to leave this world a little better than he found it."
description: "Son" is another three year man, who during this comparatively short time has made more friends than most of us make in four. He is a good student, and an athlete of no mean ability. He has scrubbed at baseball for the last two years and has been a mainstay on that team. "Son" used to be found turning his steps towards Lucy Cobb but now he has forsaken that institution and confines his reflections to the vicinity of the Capital City. It is rumored that "Son" had planned a pleasant trip to the city for Thanksgiving Day, but for reasons unknown, he remained here for the Clemson game. He pleads ignorance of such plans, so we discount the rumor at once. "Son" expects to enter the commercial world upon receiving the old dip and unless he revolutionizes his lumber business, he may turn banker. Power to you, "Son."

name: CHARLES FRANK WHITNER, JR., B.S.
motto: "Politeness and civility are the best capitals ever invested in business."
description: Charley dropped in three years ago upon our University with a motley crew that hailed from Atlanta. He is no longer among the rabble, for since arriving he has risen high among his fellow students. Though quiet and reserved, Charles has a certain attribute about him which makes all say, "To know him is to love him." It can be justly said, that his actions speak louder than his words, and his actions are always of the most commendable sort. Would that there were more like you, Charley! This young man is a student of the first magnitude, and a hard worker in general. Although not an athlete, he shines in the famous indoor sports, pool and billiards. It would not be fair to omit that C. Frank has brought joy to the hearts of many fair maidens, particularly to some of our Athens belles. We wish you success, Charley, though our wishes are really unnecessary, for you will attain whatever you strive for in this world.

name: JOHN BENSON WEIR
motto: "A face that does not smile is never fair."
enrolltime: During his four years of college life
description: After conquering "Ninety Six," "Jack" came to Georgia, and before his hair would heed the touch of a comb, he had firmly established himself with the ways of the "Classic City." During his four years of college life, "Jack" has made worlds of friends. It is quite the delight of the fair ones to have this fashion plate among the party at a dance, bridge or tea. Away from the ladies, "Jack" takes his recreation in tennis, golf and billiards, being quite a shark along these lines. Thus far he has remained true to the callings of a bachelor, but sooner or later his shining black hair will bow before his queen—such is the rumor. But we do not stop here because "Jack" is quite a student and has made an excellent record in the Engineering Department. Good luck to you, "Jack." It has been a pleasure to know you, and may success be yours in all life's undertakings.

name: BLONNIE HUGH WILLIAMS
motto: "You can fool me one time and it won't be my fault, but if a second time, it will be."
description: "B(ull) H(enry)" drove up from the plains of South Georgia. He was a bit tardy, because he had to harvest all the crop and put all his sugarcane into jugs before his Pa would let him enter the "Bull Shooting" Association. Be not hasty in passing judgment upon the participant of Old College's affairs (none excluded), but look again at him. Yes, he was an archlight, not among the "Normalites" nor any other part of the College Curriculum, but among the "Hens" who migrate here in the summer. He says very little, he talks very much; he works very little, he plays very much; yet he is never broke. There are many things "B. H." does not know how to do, but he does know how to wear out his pants and shoe soles, and how to hold a straight face under any and all circumstances. He can not do other than succeed for he is dependable.

name: OLIN STEWART WILLIS, A.B.
motto: "Mark ye well the pathway of life; ye travel it but once."
description: Here, gentlemen, is the specimen that passeth all understanding. Mr. Willis came very near keeping the Pandora out of the press since the whole staff had to go adjective hunting to trump up a wordy likeness. The one reliable phrase that did not fail us was this one: "He is a darn good fellow." When that is said about a man, the ensuing encomiums are as but liquid on the well known swan's back. We look again. We conclude that Diogenes and his famed lantern would have earned great solace by playing around in "Pug's" territory, which is just a long way to say that he is the embodiment of honesty and straightforwardness. The trait of frankness is present when we hear one of "Pug's" cohorts say that he would make a good umpire, because he calls "em like he sees 'em." We dare say that his discretion and ability will carry him to higher heights than that, though. Get in that game of Life and fight, "Pug," and we'll stake the Pandora profits that you win out.

name: WILLIAM GORDON WINGATE, B.S.A.
motto: "It is not wise to be wiser than is necessary."
enrolltime: fall of '13; fall of '18; fall of '20
description: Well, young gentlemen, we will now consider one of the famous characters of recent history at this institution. "Bill" Wingate is probably one of the oldest men in point of service in the present Senior class. He made his debut here, if it may be called such, in the fall of '13. At that time he managed to stay for one whole college year. Then the call of the clodhopper came creeping into his youthful life and he served two years on the farm. The fall of '18 found him at Georgia for his second year. The following summer "Bill" decided to do his bit toward making the world safe for the Democrats. As a result he was not able to return to Georgia until the fall of '20, and he has been here since that time getting off his degree work. This task was completed last summer so this year has found "Bill" taking some graduate work and teaching a class of Rehabs. Wingate is steady and reliable and has in him the qualities which make for success. Luck to you, "Bill."

name: Jasper Guy Woodroof
motto: "If I am useful, honest, and have a good character, what does it matter if I am not rich?"
description: "Woody" is a man of many words, very fast of speech. He has made a great record here in many ways. He has taken an active part in the religious activities of the Campus and churches, mostly of the latter. He has but three years and Summer Schools to his credit, but during these three years he has been very active in the above named organizations. He may not know how to tell jokes, but he knows how to spray trees and eat all kinds of fruit. He may be studious in Horticultural lines, but he is "arsenic" among the fairer sex, especially the summer school lassies. Though often laboring under the most trying circumstances, he has never given up. Even the surgeon's knife was not too sharp to cause this persistent character to lose courage. We look for great things from you, "Woody."

name: Herbert Emerson Woodruff
motto: "Bull and ability is a great combination, but if you can have only one—leave off ability."
description: If the Kaiser had not been overly ambitious "Woody" would have graduated with the class of '20. Although delayed two years by his tour abroad, he returned to Georgia and resumed his interrupted task with added zeal. "Woody" has excelled in debating, dabbled in politics somewhat and is a leader in Mac's Horticultural Department. His rotund figure betrays the fact that he is a regular patron of "Nicks," where he indulges excessively in Eskimo Pies. Although he is very bashful when around the young ladies, yet he was sometimes seen walking over to the "Ag" college with a certain Co-ed. "Woody" has contemplated either entering politics or growing peach trees. We predict for him success in which ever he chooses, for "Bull" and ability are a great combination, and "Woody" has both of these qualities.

name: EDWARD STEPHENS WRIGHT, B.S.
motto: "Possess self-confidence and work to some definite goal."
description: Edward Stephens Wright, Atlanta lad, unaffected, even tempered, of admirable disposition; "Ed," like the great, has not been as much appreciated by people in general as he has been by those who have worked with him intimately. Few students in the University know that he has in his make-up a mind that penetrates the outer covering and grasps the kernel of things, for he has not made himself in the least obtrusive. He has gone about his work in a manner that bespeaks the true gentleman—a man of sincerity, of honesty, and of whole-hearted good will toward the man at his elbow. "Ed" is going to assist "Mother" Nature in her care for the bodies of men and if he is as successful as a physician and surgeon as he has been in his pursuit of the atom, the protozoa, and the vertebrates, he will make the spirits of Jenner and Osler, and a host of others famous in Science sit up and take notice. A good student and a better friend is "Ed" Wright.

name: GEORGE WASHINGTON WRIGHT, JR., B.S.
motto: "Work is the first prerequisite of success."
description: According to collegiate traditions, this attenuated fellow is called "Shorty" not because he is short, but because he is long—so long that he ought to appear in installments. He is from Augusta (Ga.) and his respect for a person is in direct proportion to the number of people that person knows from his native hamlet. People, according to "Shorty," are divided into three classes: (a) those who live on the Hill; (b) those who know people who live on the Hill, and (c) barbarians. Aside from brushing his teeth, his favorite sport is to jumble a long list of chemical reactions together and watch the Ch's and Ph's do funny things. But no matter how interested he becomes, he always keeps his weather eye on the mail box, and it is estimated that if the special deliveries he gets were placed end on end, they would form a line nearly as long as his legs. "Shorty" is a good fellow, liked by all who know him, and he has our sincerest best wishes.

name: BEUNICE ADAMS, A.B.S.S.
motto: "Be good, sweet maid, let who will be clever; Do noble things; do not dream them all day long, And thus make life and death, and that vast for-ever, One grand sweet song."
description: Beunice has been with us but one year and her gentleness of manner and sweet temper have won for her a right warm place in our hearts. Beunice is the very embodiment and spirit of the above lines of Kingsley. In fact when interviewed as to her philosophy of life, what should she give but, "Get something out of life worthy of imparting to others." To the person who thinks deep and says little there is bound to come the getting of something worthy of passing on to others. With it also comes the deep understanding and appreciation that few of us possess. Beunice has reflected credit upon both Lucy Cobb Institute and the State Normal, which she attended previous to entering the University, by the splendid record that she has made in her work this year.

name: RUTH ALSTON BATES, B.S.Ed.
motto: "Things seem sweeter when we possess them not."
enrolltime: after the first year of co-education at Georgia
description: Ruth was a pioneer at Georgia. And sure she did not therein miss her calling. For does she not possess the requisites of a pioneer? Pressing toward the goal, true to her mark, possessing an insatiable love for the old camp along the newly blazed trail—such a wayfarer proved Ruth, when after the first year of co-education at Georgia, she came wandering back, hundreds of miles across Texas sands to finish her education at her Alma Mater. Her loyalty and truth spoke forth in her basketball goals, or in the many evidences of a good shot found in the middle of a bull's-eye during the rifle practice, and best of all in her sincere attitude toward all her friends. We wonder if, after she shall have entered her profession, we should be far wrong in still creating pictures of her which seem to us now so characteristic—Ruth full of life and enthusiasm, hitting the gypsy trail in much the same manner as she headed the hikes of our University Y. W. C. A.

name: Marguerite Moore Beale
motto: "I want what I want when I want it, and I know what I want right well."
description: Marguerite, of whom nothing more need be said than, "To know her is to love her," came to Georgia two years ago, a graduate of the State Normal. She entered the University filled with many lofty ambitions, which she, triumphing over every obstacle but remaining withal, the same lovable character, has realized during her two years of study here. Attached to her name are some of the highest honors possible for a student to attain, but greater than all of these is her true friendship and love for every one which are expressed even in the smallest acts of her every day life. Marguerite, your ideals are high, may they always remain so in the after years when you shall look back upon your Alma Mater as the inspiration of many of them.

name: Ruby Marie Bell
motto: "Not to be influenced by human frailties but to look steadily toward an ideal."
description: Here is a lady who should have inhabited the old sphere some centuries ago. Not that she is an old fashioned girlie—she isn't—but because she is such a student and a lover of the writers of ye olden days that to hear her talk you would think she knew them all personally—why she never puts "Miss" before the names of the Muses—yet she is the essence of refinement and culture. Ruby Marie has been one of the most zealous members of the Zodiac Club—if you want to hear about it and its activities just ask her. In all things just as in this, she puts her whole heart and soul. To have her behind a thing means its going thru; in other words, she's dependable, a truly enviable thing to have said about one; if you can think of anything better she truly merits it!

name: Ina Elbin Cooper
motto: "Just try to hold fast to the highest ideals that float across my vision in moments of exaltation."
description: Don't get it into your head that there will ever be a day when Miss Cooper can be called "old"! Possessing the keenest sense of humor and just bubbling over with fun and wit, she will always be young and an entertaining companion. Her characteristics and descriptions of people and incidents are as clever and spontaneously witty as any Mark Twain can offer. Maybe it is a little difficult to let little boys wash their faces in Spanish, but she can make so many timely remarks about it that those sitting near her get convulsed with laughter. There is one thing that she has made up her mind to do and it will be the best bet that she will do it—that is to pass up French without learning Pro. Lustrat's French Verb Book by heart. Under this cloak of wit is a sterling character, kind, sympathetic, and bearing nobly her share of the world's responsibilities, Miss Cooper is a genuine and delightful woman.

name: Allee Elizabeth Dorsey
motto: "The less I speak, the more I meditate."
description: Hi! What's the thrill today? Anybody who knew Elizabeth and heard from just around the corner the above words would know they were addressed to her. Of all the people we ever knew, she has the most thrills—this year she has thrilled over the Homecon Club as its President, thrilled over Chi Omega and thrilled over Alpha Mu, and thrilled over—oh! well she wouldn't want his name mentioned just now! Elizabeth is always "tea-hounding" yet she always has those lessons up, even tho the most brilliant social event of the season took place the night before. Next year she will certainly be missed off the Campus. Her ever ready smile and word of cheer has been a source of inspiration to teachers and students alike. We hate to bid you farewell, may you have much success in the adventures of life, and we know whatever you do, wherever you go, you will reflect credit on your beloved Alma Mater—Old Georgia.

name: ANNA BELLE DRAKE, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Give to the world the best you have, unmindful of the returns."
description: Behold! The Solomon of the Home Economics girls—in the chemical world she is a real wonder—Alpha amino Beta phenol propionic acids, etc., etc., hold no terror for her. Madame Curie, you had better look out if you don't want to come down from your coveted pedestal—not only does Anna Belle stand first in her classes but also in the first ranks in the hearts of those who know her best. She is quiet and unassuming in her manner, yet possessor of a personality with an undercurrent that once you are engulfed within its flow you are held willingly. Indeed she makes a wonderful pal. Anna Belle, we are dreaming big dreams for you and out in the big field of life we have visions of them coming into realization—there's no reason why you shouldn't reach the top. "Hitch your wagon to a star," and while you pull we are pushing. Bon voyage!

name: KATHLEEN DRAKE, A.B.Ed.
motto: "Even the heaviest burden grows lighter when borne with a smile."
description: Although it is sometimes said that intellectuality and coldness go hand in hand; we who know Kathleen realize that is not the case with her. In the two short years that she has been with us she has made her impress on the intellectual life of the University and her gracious word and cheerful smile have lent much to the brightening of our lives. Here is the artistic nature interested in the beautiful myths of long ago and in Cupid particularly, although that Cupid is just as real as is an instructor of our own campus who frequently makes his appearance at the door of the psychological laboratory and other convenient places when she is about. Yet, in spite of this impractical phase of her nature, she has the force of character to bring about the desired end, and the real worth that makes us feel glad that we know her and that she is one of our number.

name: LULA BLANCHE EDWARDS
motto: "If you can't be a moss-gatherer, don't be a stone thrower."
enrolltime: two years record at the University of Georgia
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet, '21 and 22
description: The State Normal School has furnished us with another of those priceless jewels who this time is none other than Lula Edwards. She needs no write-up to tell of her two years record at the University of Georgia, for do not the honors that her fellow students have bestowed upon her bespeak the confidence and esteem that she has won while here? She has been faithful and diligent in all that she has undertaken. As President of the Student Council, she has shown love for the girls and a sympathetic understanding of their problems as University women. Lula says that if you can not be a moss gatherer, do not be a stone thrower, and she lives up to this, too, for she speaks only good of others. "None knew her but to love her, none named her but to praise."

name: NANCY GURR
motto: "If I were loved as I desire to be, this world would be a paradise to me."
enrolltime: two years that she has been at Georgia
description: Nancy came to us from the State Normal School with an enviable record and she has been leading her classmates a rather rapid race in trying to keep apace with her during the two years that she has been at Georgia. In fact Madam Gossip would have it that Nancy is somewhat a shark when it comes to chemistry and a few other scientific courses, and if certain popular week-end past times among some of the Co-eds be termed scientific, I'm sure that Nancy ranks A plus. A casual glance at this quiet, unassuming classmate would indicate that she is just an ordinary co-ed, but closer acquaintance reveals to one that the mechanism of Nancy's general makeup is very extraordinary. In fact it is so unusual that there are no few guesses as to her future career. Whatever line of future activity may be her choice there can be no doubt of a wonderful success for her. She has the best and sincerest wishes of her classmates.

name: QUEEN ELIZABETH HOLDEN, A.B.S.S.
motto: "The secret of success is constancy to purpose."
description: Behold and ponder well, "Queen of the Co-eds"! This charming young lady with the smiling brown eyes and equally brown bobbed hair made her advent among us about a year ago, but even in that short time has clearly evinced her right to the aforesaid title. Queen is very reticent in regard to her love affairs and consequently little is known of them. However, she has been known to make several trips to Virginia lately—to Randolph-Macon, of course (?). Among the many charming qualities of this lady's personality are her absolute sincerity and whole-heartedness, qualities rarely met with in the modern American girl. Tho not at all desirous of academic honors, "Queen" has made for herself a very creditable name in scholastic circles. This fact, together with the many energetic characteristics of her nature, points to a large measure of success in whatever path of life she may choose to take—a path paved all the way with the best wishes of her many sincere friends.

name: MARY HUNTER MARTIN, A.B.S.S.
motto: "Success treads on the heels of every right effort."
enrolltime: two years work at the University
description: Mary is an Athens girl and one of the representatives of the State Normal School. She has done her two years work at the University in high style and has shown a special proclivity for pulling down "100s" in Zoo—an achievement very few people can boast of. A more conscientious student, whether in classroom or in outside activities, has not attended the University. When you want anything done, if you ask Mary, she will be right there with the goods. Mary possesses an unusual soft and pleasing voice and with her quiet and gentle ways has won her place in the esteem and affection of all. She is a good sport and always ready to enter in and have a good time. We are not in a position to say what Mary's plans for the future are, but we feel mighty sure that they will be carried out and in a way that will be of greatest service. Mary, may you always have the best of good fortune, is our wish.

name: WILMA JUANITA MITCHELL
motto: "So live that when you die the undertaker will be sorry."
clubs: Vice-President of Y. W. C. A.
Vice-President of House Council of Woman's Building
description: She's not much to look at, "much" referring to quantity, not quality, but looking long and hard you perceive that here is a little bunch of nerves that mean all pep and go. In the course of a week she uses enough energy to run a good sized locomotive a long way, but we'll wager it's well used. She likes a good time but she is not frivolous; witness her very serious pursuit of the Art and Science of Home Economics. Her training here is in the right direction, and some day we expect to see published from her pen some book as to "The Care and Keeping of the Methodist Parsonage." Well, here's joy and happiness, Billy.

name: ANNE RUTH MOORE
motto: "Variety is the spice of life—the spicer the better."
description: A vivacious smile for old and young alike, a gentle disposition, a magnetic ability for attracting the elusive first letter of the alphabet, and a friendly willingness to help every one with whom she comes in contact, must distinguish Anne Ruth from the ordinary girl. It is a distinct surprise to the stranger to hear Anne Ruth talk learnedly about stocks and bonds, insurance and Wall Street transactions, until he realizes her unlimited knowledge of such subjects, apparently so foreign to her loving nature. She will make a perfect secretary for some rich business man. Truly marvelous is this winsome young lady's propensity for bootlicking the teachers, even in the physics department, as was demonstrated by the acquisition of a tiny compass for her watch strap. The campus will miss Anne Ruth's cheerful greeting and cordial manner next year, but she will long be remembered here as one of the brightest and most attractive co-eds that ever entered the old iron arch way.

name: MARION BAUDRY MOORE
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil o'er books consumed the midnight oil?"
description: Where did she hail from, this bunch of good natured sunshine that we call Marion? She always greets the fellow students with one of those enticing, contagious smiles that comes no matter what happens. Is there any wonder that she has so many admirers among the opposite sex? This smile is not the only admirable quality that Marion possesses that many are envious of, for who has ever seen the fair Marion with her nose in a book any later than twelve P. M., and yet who outranks her in classes? She has made a very fine record for herself while at Georgia. There is nothing that she undertakes that she cannot accomplish. Loved by those who know her best, liked by others who know her less, but admired by all, is the best way of expressing the popularity and esteem that Marion has won during her two years stay at Georgia. It was Randolph-Macon's loss and our gain when she came to us in her Junior year.

name: IDA ELIZABETH POUND
motto: "Happiness comes with fulfillment of duty."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Women's Student Government Council, '22
description: Nobody has to be asked to say good things about Ida Pound, the good things must be said because a person would have to be deaf, dumb and blind not to recognize that she is eminently worth while. Those who know her admire her because she stands for the best and highest in womanhood. She is true, just and dependable. She has the strongest convictions and opinions and yet she is big enough to "get the other fellow's viewpoint." This old world would certainly be a more comfortable and pleasant place if there were more daughters, students and citizens like Ida Pound. She has a most enviable record for scholarship, being one of the two to record the highest grades in the senior class of '22. She is an alumna that even a school as renowned as the University of Georgia will be proud to claim.

name: FRANCES BAKER SIMPSON, B.S.H.E.
motto: "Modesty is the chief virtue."
clubs: Vice-President of Student Government Association of Women, '22
description: Sh!! Sh-h-h!! You probably think a tornado is bursting in your door, to find that it is only Frances, our House Council President, who believes in doing everything on the strict q. t., but who incidentally makes more noise than the objects of her "shoos." (N. B.-Said objects are not chickens, for such an incident as the above often takes place around mid-night in the "Co-ed barn.") To see Frances walk across the campus is to know that she is full of "pep" and enthusiasm and that whatever that bundle of energy gets behind in life, it is bound to make rapid progress forward. Frances comes to us from Agnes Scott, where she left an enviable record and her two years' course at Georgia has been attended by high marks from her "Profs" and respect from her fellow students. Her sunny nature has won for her a warm place in the hearts of University students of both sexes.

name: MARY ELIZABETH WHITE, B.S.H.E.
motto: "It is the patient holding out, that makes the winner win."
clubs: State Prize Essay-"The Great Seal of Georgia"
description: Who is Mary? Why Mary is our essayist and she hasn't won as many prizes yet as she's going to in the future. Her enviable college career is only half of what you think. Yes, she's studious, makes good grades and all of that, but she's an expert along other lines as well. Ask her if she ever uses her mind for purposes such as will trap us foolish ones with a catchy problem. As for thoughtfulness! She always thinks of things like smuggling you a piece of toast on a cold morning when your alarm accidently didn't go off on time or bringing your mail if she happens to be passing by. All in all, Mary is made of fine stuff and whatever she determines to do "it is to be," so knowing that she will determine to do only the finest, our corolary is-Georgia will some day be even more boastful of being Mary's Alma Mater.

name: Robert Lanier Anderson, Jr.
motto: "Achievement is the crown of effort and the diadem of thought."
description: Ladies and gentlemen, clear the stage for one "Lonnie" Anderson, the central city's favorite. There are only two men in college who have ever seen his hair much shorter than at present, and one of them walked the lowers of a freshman with him. We must take notice that "Lonnie" hasn't spent six years in the University for nothing. During this time he has won two degrees and well does he deserve them. He has been quite an exponent on the Glee and Mandolin Club and entertained thousands of Georgia's Citizens with the charm of his box. "Lonnie," we have been honored by your association and fellowship. Your kind disposition and earnestness of purpose make us love you. Continue in your good works and all will be well. Accept our wishes for a great career.

name: Allen Johnson Arnold
motto: "A life well lost is better than a death well won."
description: Great joy tempered with some slight consternation reigned in the metropolis of Monroe on September 3, 1899, A. D. There was joy for the populace knew that a great barrister had been given to the world. "Hap" is more or less a landmark around the University, this being his fifth year. This June he will return to Monroe, with his LL.B. tucked under his arm, there to hang out his shingle in the practice of his chosen profession. "Hap" is rather quiet, which at first gave us the impression of bashfulness, but we soon found our mistake, and that he is an example of the saying that, "Still water runs deep." But coming down to facts, "Hap" is one of the most likeable men in his class, always ready to do any thing he can for you—he makes one of the best friends imaginable. "Hap" enters everything with an enthusiasm that is bound to carry him far.

name: BUNAH B. BOWERS, LL.B.
motto: "I am afraid of nothing on earth, or above the earth, or under the earth, but to do wrong."
description: With Bunk's exit the Colonial Theatre and Georgian Hotel loses their best patron. A more profound student and disciple of S'vanus, the University can never boast. It is rumored that he once attended a Saturday class, but the same has never appeared to be of record. He is reputed to have once taken voice at Lucy Cobb, but this also does not appear to be on record. However, he is one of the foremost "tenors" of the Law Department choir, and just where he acquired this rare "tenor" is as uncertain as his age. A past master in the art of speculation and known "at one fell swoop of tick or tape" to have cured himself of a chronic disease, by the simple process of elimination. We know not his plans for the future, but his thorough mastery of the Blackstonian principles should render him able to procure for himself a place in the "limelight" in his future endeavors.

name: REUBEN ALVIN BRASWELL, LL.B.
motto: "Get the principal things of life, and do not digress from these for unimportant things."
description: Company, halt! Page the Junior Senator from Georgia, for we now have with us a most ardent exponent of his doctrines. Do you crave an argument, well, question the integrity of this, his political hero, and he will bear with you at any time, any place and at any length, you choose the weapons. After giving the "German boys" a thorough thrashing, his attitude toward the next war is "there ain't going to be none." Since we have known him he has been an industrious and hard working student and one of Georgia's best. Every inch sincere in his beliefs, he allows no statement to go unchallenged, he brooks no opposition from whatever source and however dire may be the consequences. Inasmuch as he expects every man to do his duty, he never refuses a helping hand to any one. True to his friends, just towards his adversaries, we foresee in him a demagogue of parties but an untiring statesman and an idol of clients.

name: JOSEPH ANDREW BROWN, LL.B.
motto: "The path of duty I shall endeavor to travel, fearing no evil and dreading no consequences."
description: The distinguished looking gentleman herewith has the honor of calling Athens his home. He is one of the many sons who have sought to attain the higher realms of the professional world thru the instrumentality of the Lumpkin Law School. Though "Buster" has never stayed on the campus he has made it his adopted home on many occasions—he may frequently be found in Old College Hall of Fame, engaged in an animated conversation with the "Immortal Triumvirate" the inhabitants thereof "Messrs. Hayes, Braswell and Few." But aside from this one digression from the trodden paths of duty, "Buster" is rarely ever seen unless busily occupied in the analysis of some complicated legal problem. His friends are numbered by his acquaintances, and what more can be asked! With his cheerful and alert mind, "Buster" will never lack clients and no jury will be able to resist his earnest appeal. Good luck, "Buster," and may Dame Fortune accompany you down the pathways of life.

name: NEPHEW KING CLARK, LL.B.
motto: "The greatest errors are committed and the most judgment is shown in the choice of individuals."
description: After deserting the sunny south to prep at Lawrenceville, and thence via V. M. I. for two years, Nevy returned to his native state to study law. Nevy is a many-sided man; he is a master in the art of making friends, a faithful student, one of our most brilliant social butterflies, and is looked upon as being one of the most popular men in the University. After doffing the cap and gown with his LL.B. to encourage him, Nevy expects to descend the ladder of preferment in Savannah. We have confidence that if he can convince a jury as easily as he can obtain an affirmative answer from the fair sex, the future generations will abide by Judge Clark's decisions. In conclusion, be it said that Nevy numbers as his friends all who know him. To a man who can make friends so easily and hold them so endearingly, we predict a happy life.

name: MITCHELL SETH DEKLE, LL.B.
motto: "Let justice be done tho' the heavens fall."
description: Battalions, attention! Rest! Behold in the foreground, gentlemen, Wellington's only equal, Stonewall Jackson's only rival, our friend, classmate, and Cadet Colonel, Mitchell Seth Dekle. "Deecie" hails from the wilds of Southern Georgia, where Atlantic's balmy breezes are said to rustle the jungles around the metropolis of Metter. Seth has acquired a happy faculty of guessing, has polished it into a fine art; and we are informed (reliably) that this accounts largely for his record in law. "Be true to your convictions." Seth likes to play ball, and had it not been that "Silvey" coaxed him to devote himself more assiduously to the legal pursuit, otherwise, Tyrus and Ruth, but more especially the big moundsmen, would be trembling for their tottering fame. He is a good student as his list of honors demonstrate, also conscientious and consistent, and we predict for him, upon his return to the regions of Southeast Georgia, friends, success and wealth.

name: WALTER HUGH DONOHUE, LL.B.
motto: "Work—a companion in all of our struggles."
description: Yes, he is from Savannah, and a more jovial Irishman there never was. His fame as a dispeller of gloom has spread far and wide and he is panacea for all ills. "Mike" is another member of the renowned Law School quartet and is said to have amassed quite a fortune because of his dexterity in emitting harmonious discords. 'Tis said of "Mike" that though he has been in Athens for the past three years, his heart has been in Savannah. If this is true, we can account for the "pep" that he has displayed, for it is for such as that men perform their noblest deeds. "Mike," we will carry with us always the memory of your quaint "Howdie," and your spirit of optimism will spur us onward to greater deeds. We have had the pleasure and privilege of hearing "Mike" argue cases in Moot Court, and if the earnestness of knowledge which characterizes his work, there is incorporated into his work in the future his success as a criminal lawyer seems assured.

name: Samuel Warren Few
motto: "In thoughts sublime that pierce the night like stars, and with their mild persistence urge man's search to vaster issues."
description: "Seldom" hails from the broom and sage fields around Apalachee, and they even say that he followed a pair of plows back of a train into Athens. We know him mainly for his "partnership" with Braswell and Haynes; and we are told; he is the co-principal in many unfathomable maneuvers staged in the law library. In spite of the handicap of his environment (aforesaid partnership), he has emerged with delinquency, in two courts: first, he positively and emphatically states that he is constitutionally opposed to wearing a derby and carrying a cane. (What a metamorphosis! Last Sunday, seen with a cane, and a Jane.) Second, that he is desperately and passionately in love. Allegation vehemently denied, but circumstantially proved. He came to us victim of "co-edu-phobia;" but now he leaves afflicted with "co-edu-mania." In fact, he has not missed a single performance at the Colonial during his sojourn here. But "Seldom" has made the University a good student and will return to give Georgia a higher gift of citizenship. May the satisfaction purchased by thy resolves be the crowning reward of a life well spent.

name: John Dewey Godfrey
motto: "Life is a compromise; a mean between extremes; not all you expect but not as bad as it could be."
description: "Jack" is entitled to much praise, because in the short space of two years he has risen from the humble position of being the "target" of Dr. Morris, to a very high and exalted location, that of his "righthand man." Aside from this meritorious accomplishment, he shares first honor with Col. Gray in the contest for Premier dispenser of Jokes in the University. "Jack" may be truthfully called the Beau Brummel of the Law School, and with his "doiby" and flaring bow ties, he cuts a wide swath among the fair sex of Athens and adjoining towns. He is also a singer of note, and his warbling may be heard daily by all who enter the law building. "Jack" is a student who is not content with mere preparation of his classroom work, but may be found nightly laboriously delving into the reports, assisting a certain local attorney in the preparation of his briefs and other documents. This interest continued will be amply rewarded.

name: EDWARD JAMES GOODWIN, LL.B.
motto: "Make books thy tools, but be not a servant to them."
description: Ladies and gentlemen, we have with us today a disciple of Fishnu, the Great, whose chief occupation is pounding out "jazz" either on the piano in the rear of Kress' famous emporium, or upon the poor, decrepit waste basket in the Senior classroom. But truthfully, "Goody" is a musician of first water, and his services are always in great demand. When a freshman, he was noted for his many eccentricities, chief of which was his indulgence in the habit of carrying lead pipes concealed in the folds of his umbrella. "Goody's" better judgment prevailed here and these were cast aside for the more serious thoughts of life. 'Tis said that he has a way all his own with the ladies, and of this we are quite certain. But laying all jokes aside, "Goody" is a conscientious worker, and one who stands high in the esteem of the entire student body. To know him is to like him, and this combined with his legal lore, causes us to predict for him a brilliant future.

name: HERBERT CLINTON GRAY, LL.B.
motto: "Jewels have points that wound us; fortune has fetters that sting—Better the dream of a beggar than the discontent of a king."
clubs: Charter Member of Connely Post Disabled Veterans of the World War
description: This young fellow is another of Augusta's own. The University has been immensely benefited because of his sojourn within its circle. His example as a student has been a marvelous inspiration to his fellows and his accomplishments in every way are highly gratifying to his professors. They would mourn for the day of his parting if it were not for the bigger game in life to be played after college days have faded into one of the memories of the past. Herbert, a glad hand goes with you and the highest hopes for a great success at the bar shall ever be present in the hearts of your classmates. Let us hear from you, for we are expecting the best and are satisfied that nothing short of happiness awaits you. Adieu! Adieu!

name: WESLEY THOMAS HARGRETT, JR.
motto: "Eat, drink and be merry."
clubs: First Sergeant S. A. T. C.
description: It was back in 1917, many years before the soldiers' bonus had been thought of, or statesmen had conceived the idea of a four-power Pacific pact, that this stately looking specimen of Georgia manhood, familiarly known as "Wes," entered the University. And during the five years that he has been here, he has made his presence known. In every phase of college activities, he has ranked as a leader. On the diamond, grid, and even in the ballroom, he has been a favorite. "Wes" has been taking law at the University. Whether or not this means that he is to become a lawyer is yet to be determined. But regardless of what phase of life's activities he may enter, we believe that he will be a favorite there also. Best wishes, old man!

name: JULIAN HARTRIDGE
motto: "Let the other fellow worry."
description: Kind reader, turn not this page in haste. Glance upon the beaming countenance of the inimitable Julian, and then learn of the man. He hails from one of the suburbs of Pooler, Georgia, known as Savannah. After leading a hectic life under the Stars and Stripes in the A. E. F., shooting the big gun in the field artillery, he decided to adopt as the arbiter of his destiny one "Silvy" of the Lumpkin Law School. He still leads a hectic life, but turned to the more peaceful pastime of shooting the bull. He has won many friends during his three years' stay at the University and has made a fine record. After grabbing his "dip," his intentions are to become a pillar of the law on his native heath, and thus to assist the prohibition department to run the bootlegger out of Savannah into jail. He has shown marked ability in the study of his chosen profession, and the best wishes of his friends for success and good luck will always be with him.

name: JONES HAYES, LL.B.
motto: "I envy no man that knows more than myself, but pity them that know less."
description: Here he is, fellows, the man who mistook a spell of chronic laziness for a call to practice law. No man ever made a happier exit from the tutelage of the learned "Doctor," and so great is the Dr.'s affection for him, and vice versa, that he has by a special request taken a number of his courses twice. As a consequence he has captured the coveted degree of LL.B. He has been called upon to discuss the various phases of the law with a frequency approximating three million times, and it is said that he once announced ready. Aside from his diligent activities in the Law Department he is frequently seen engaged in "tossing the Taurus" around old College's back yard. But this is simply a display of Red's outstanding characteristic, an unbounded love for the sport of the toreador. He is indeed a true friend, sincere, amiable, and exemplary. His college career has been marked by a grim determination which will count much for success.

name: RICHARD LEE HICKEY, LL.B.
motto: "When in doubt, keep on talking."
enrolltime: The class of twenty-one lost a man, that twenty-two was glad to get, when Hick changed his course from Academic to Law.
description: Hard working, conscientious, ever willing to do his part, Richard has indeed passed his years successfully in the University. His genial personality, ready smile and quick wit, have won for him many friends, and wherever known he is genuinely liked. Along with his work he has distinguished himself and the University as an eminent golfer of premier caliber. He has placed well in several Southern and National Championships, and in 1921 won the amateur title of Atlanta. His ability as a lawyer is one that is sure to attain the height of success, and make for him a record that will be admired by all who follow the profession. It will be mighty hard to tell you good-bye, old man, because you have been a friend good and true, but we are sure that you will show the same in your future life, that you have shown at Georgia.

name: Milton Lee Holcombe
motto: "A thing of beauty is a joy forever."
description: The accompanying physiognomy represents one of the most modest and retiring gentlemen that ever matriculated at the University. In his three years stay among us. "Along the cool sequestrated vale of life He kept the even tenor of his ways." Mr. Holcombe believes that success comes not alone to the noisy but likewise to the quiet. It is rumored by those who know him best that he yearns to change his present happy mode of existence and join the benedicts of the silent majority. This rumor, however, we do not vouch for. Upon leaving these sacred precincts, our friend will take up the practice of law. Where he will take it we are not informed. He may in course of time achieve the distinction of a justice of the peace and hold the unlettered minds in gaping awe as words of wisdom trickle from his immortal lips. Best wishes. "Silence is golden."

name: Harvey John Kennedy
motto: "Say something if you have to take it back."
description: From the little village of Barnesville, lo and behold "a Daniel is come to judgment." From the poppy-covered fields of France he returned to the field of carnage of the Lumpkin Law School. A young man of sterling qualities, and a real gentleman, he has made his path through the University notable in many respects. His list of honors shows that his activities have covered divers lines. They range from the social activities of the Buccaneers and oratorical successes, to being leader of the Junior class and of the local American Legion Post, to say nothing of being a shining light in the Barristers Club. His nature has made a friend for him of all who know him. His scholastic record is one of the best in his department, and we feel sure that highest success will crown his efforts. May the best of luck be with you, Harvey, always.

name: Linton Grady Lanier, LL.B.
motto: "To imitate others is to confess your weakness beware; you alone are the actor in the great drama which will be completed at life's exit."
clubs: Henry W. Grady Public Speaking Club
description: "Sidney" came to us from Emory, where he was a student in arts, but on reaching Georgia he had decided that just one more good lawyer was needed, and working with that in view has succeeded wonderfully well. He is a consistent student and we believe one who possesses more "stickability" per cubic centimeter than any other man in college, and with this essential requirement for success the future can have in store for him only the choicest of nature's riches. "Sidney" plays the violin well and we have also heard it said that he plays equally well upon the heartstrings of the opposite sex. And who could dispute it, after having once gazed upon his radiant countenance? Our class appreciates you, "Sidney," and we feel quite sure that within a few years the old home town will be appreciating you fully as much but calling you their own "Judge" Lanier.

name: Albert Theodore Levie, LL.B.
motto: "May there be no moaning at the BAR when I put out to sea."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Law Class
description: The limiting of the desires of the right kind of ambition are two of the issues in the life of the successful. "Ted" has instilled both in his makeup: Self-denial, that he may serve others, and ambition of noble purpose. His record as a disciple of the law borders on the brilliant and with a better spirit than Polonius he has bound the hearts of his comrades to him. He has the vision the ability and the motives that dominated the genuinely great of the Empire State and fortunate will be the men and the women, the boys and the girls with whom he labors. He knows nothing of ostentation but is charged with the spirit of a man.

name: JAMES PETER McDOWELL, LL.B.
motto: "We who live for ourselves accomplish nothing."
description: We, who in the past three years have become more intimately acquainted with the gentleman of the accompanying likeness, take pleasure in introducing him as a friend. If you have never been to Griffin, but have met "Mac," you'll naturally believe it must be a good place. Provided that college life be a fair index to ultimate success; then "Mac" has the scales already balanced in his favor. By glancing at his list of honors reminds us that he's been a leader in everything attempted. He does things well, and has a way of gaining the confidence and respect of his fellows—a way of doing what he sets out to do. Does this seem extraordinary? No, not when you are referred to his philosophy of life, quoted below. Now can you understand why those who know him best have bound him to themselves with hoops of steel, and wish him many happy returns for a life well spent!

name: THEO JACKSON McGEE, LL.B.
motto: "Know this: In some undertakings you will succeed, and in some fail. Know this and be a man in each."
enrolltime: Coming to Athens three years ago
description: All hail the original "tenant in possession of the Lumpkin Law School," "T. J." is a man of varied business experiences, having been employed in various offices, from that of a railroad up to that of his excellency the Governor of the State of Georgia. Coming to Athens three years ago, he took his abode in Candler Hall with his established companion, Thomas. Then he was transferred to the law building, where he has been in "custodia legis" of the forgotten legal case. A man of very pleasing personality, a friend to all and esteemed highly by all who know him. "Mac" passed the bar early in his career as a student and has since been conspicuous as a promising attorney in several legal controversies. A leader of men, a thorough and consistent worker, confident in his ability, we are compelled to predict for him a brilliant career before the bar of this state.

name: EDWIN ARIEL McWHORTER, A.B., LL.B.
motto: "So live in the Present that the Past will have no regrets, the Future no fears."
description: Hail to "Block Head," the man without a doubt! This lad comes to us from Savannah. He entered the University in the fall of '16 and the six years spent here have well settled upon his record not a less fitting than the one with which we greet him. Every day of his college life he has been a blooming success. He has won almost every honor possible to be had; has gained admiration of the faculty; respect of the student body, and last but not least, "they" are thinking of building a little nest. The good wishes of every member of your class go with you, "Block;" may your good works bring you many happy returns. We hope to hear from you in the future, for we are expecting great things of you.

name: ANDREW JOSEPH RYAN, LL.B.
motto: "Smile always. One salt tear in the cup of woe, the drink it makes more bitter."
description: The number of trips that "Vacation Joe" makes each year to the Free State of Chatham is equivalent to the number of miles between here and there. The reason for these sudden and uproarious departures has never been discovered officially, but 'tis said to be in response to the beckoning of some fair queen. His high average in class work stands as a tribute to the diligence with which he has applied himself while with us. His supply of jokes is everlasting, and his daily greeting, "Say, boys, have you heard this one," will always be remembered. It has been reported that "Vacation Joe" is planning to desert the profession of law and follow the dollar over some other route. We hope this will not come to pass, for the bar will lose a very reliable man. Stay with us, old man, for we are sure that with your knowledge and personality you will become an invaluable asset to the Empire State of the South.

name: James Chalmers Shelor, LL.B.
motto: "No man can rise to true greatness unless he be tolerant of the opinions of others."
description: Here he is, gentlemen, commonly known as "Jake," but to a secluded set as "Jay-ko." He is another one of our Atlanta boys, but very different in nature as pertains to the opposite sex. A more violent denunciator of women was never created. Jake has been among the most faithful of athletes, even to the expense of his roommates—in appropriating their bed for dumb-bells and pillows for punch-bags. Aside from this he has been one of the outstanding figures during his three years' career at the University. A rare combination of good athlete, excellent student and a prince of a fellow. He is a quick thinker, a conscientious worker and exerts an honest effort in all of his undertakings. In the not far distant future, we predict that the bar of Georgia will feel his strength as a lawyer.

name: John Walter Sheppard, A.B., LL. B.
motto: "Be brief and to the point; for he who can account for his words and acts, is the owner of a priceless gem."
clubs: Secretary Jewett Williams Post, American Legion
description: At this point, gentlemen, we take great pleasure in presenting for your approval one John Walter Sheppard. To one who has been around this institution for only a comparatively short time it seems that John has been here ever since the time when the "memory of man runneth not to the contrary." During these seven years' stay among the halls of learning he has absorbed everything in two courses, and then, like Alexander, wept for more to conquer. He has gone about his work with a systematic and business like attitude and his quiet and unassuming personality has won for him friends by the scores. We are expecting big things of you, John, Chief Justice of the Supreme Court. Who knows? Remember we are for you, and here's luck. Go after them, boy, and don't forget to take the kodak along.

name: WILLIAM GLENN THOMAS, LL.B.
motto: "It isn't the time that we spend in college but the advantage we take of our opportunities."
clubs: President Henry W. Grady Speaking Society
description: "Judge" came to us three years ago from the wiregrass regions of South Georgia, and in a short time he had endeared himself to us all. During this period he has risen from an occupant of Candler Hall to the lucrative position of "tenant-in-common" of the Lumpkin Law School. What better evidence is needed of his business sagacity? "Judge" is a baseball player of note, and though he has won many games, for some unknown reason he would never sign a contract, but at present it is rumored that he is planning to sign a life contract—nuff sed! A more zealous and conscientious worker is hard to be found, and his every effort has carried with it a certain sincerity of purpose. Judging by the past he will go forth into his life's work "strong in will, to strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield."

name: HARVEY HENRY TISINGER, B.S.C., LL.B.
motto: "If you manage to keep your own respect you needn't worry about that of others; you'll have it."
description: "Ty" or "Honest Harvey," as he is known from the days of old, has been at the University six years. Since the admission of women into the sacred halls he has been converted from a confirmed hater to an ardent lover of the fair sex. Upon completing his commercial course he intended to enter the business world, but hearing the cry for better lawyers, he resolved to accept the higher calling. His record in various activities for these six years places him among the leaders of the University. And if we were to predict, we would mark his destiny unparalleled in noble achievements.

name: J. Frank Troutman, LL.B.
motto: "A good judge decides fairly, preferring equity to strict law."
description: Again the circle of Georgia's distinguished and loyal alumni finds a worthy addition to its ranks, said addition hailing from the Peach Kingdom of Fort Valley. Indeed, Frank, so reserved in nature and dignified in hearing, can well be called a "peach product," for he is as cherry and pleasant as the fairest blossoms, and as seasoned and wholesome as the maturest peach that Georgia can boast. His serious mindedness, his grave way of assuming responsibilities, and his dominant qualities of leadership have won for him the name of "Chief." In the pursuit of his professional career, Frank bids to make a level-headed counsellor and an able prosecutor. With his intensely practical nature, he can draw up an excellent brief, with his dignity he should favorably impress a jury, and with his smooth flow of eloquence and interesting way of telling things should convince the most obstinate of judges. Yet "Chief" loves his jest and is a regular fellow. A host of friends wish him the best of luck.

name: Miller Carswell Walton, LL.B.
motto: "A measly chance is a good one—only death is certain."
description: This guy, Walton, comes to us from Augusta, and it may be added that he brings all the good graces of that little village with him. He only spent three years in the University, having entered the Law School in the fall of '19. All problems of a Blackstonian nature have found and enveloped this lad, but only to be subdued and overcome after a matter of time. They all look the same to him.
