The workings of a master mind and untiring effort have not fallen short of the requirements to meet the standard of excellence set out in his course. He has acquitted himself in a splendid manner. It would be all but fair to let this gentleman pass without making a final bow to the fair sex of our good city. By his usual winning way, they have "flopped" until he has a string of admirers creditable to any man, and June 21st will find hearts not so light, nor spirits so gay, for, alas, Miller has gone away.

name: JOHN BRYAN WILSON
motto: "He has achieved success, whose life has been an inspiration and whose memory a benediction."
description: We have before us now "J. Stitt," the elongated gentleman from Loganville. During his college career "Stitt" has not attained the "rep" of being a bookworm, but has striven for the higher things in life. He not only leaves a scholastic record that many of us would envy, but has been the outstanding figure in campus leadership and graft. He is not a social artist, but few people have more ability as a mixer and friend-maker. There are three things we have against "Stitt," his opposition to co-education, his close association with "Pistol Jenkins," and the other we dare not tell, but even with these serious drawbacks, "Stitt" should develop into one of Georgia's leading legal lights.

name: RUSSELL EDWIN BLANCHARD, Ph.G.
motto: "If fame comes after death, I am in no hurry for it."
description: Observe, ladies and gentlemen, this lanky lad from Leah, Georgia. It may be pretty hard to find his home town on the map, but your first impression upon gazing at him for a moment is, that if "Blanch" is a specimen of what they grow there, the town must be all right. Staunch and loyal to the G. O. P. party, he takes meticulous care to stay eligible in Demosthenian Society so that he may vote for his man when campus politics are on the boom. He is a shining light in Old College's basketball team, which, during the season, practices nightly around ten-thirty or eleven, the players clad in anything from B. V. D.'s down. Blanchard has demonstrated most convincingly that a man can become adapted to any profession, for this natural born "rail splitter" has turned out to be a genius as compounder of drugs. He has the distinction of shooting Dr. Wilson for a round hundred, something never before accomplished in the pharmacy department. Fulfill your highest ambition, "Blanch." Marry, settle down and be happy.

name: ANDREW BLOEMICK, Ph.G.
motto: "Obstacles are not only inevitable but they are an indispensable part of the training for honorable success."
clubs: officer in the Dutch Merchant Marine
description: Glad, indeed, are we to have as classmate and friend this honest-faced young globe trotter who, during his two years at the University has had one ever present problem, and that: how to curb his restlessness long enough to get a degree; but this he has done, and in good fashion, too. After having been educated in Amsterdam for a life at sea, he was an officer in the Dutch Merchant Marine, and for two years roamed the seven seas; then, thirsting for more knowledge, he came to the University of Georgia. What he has done here is nothing short of miraculous. Mastering the English language with ease, he launched into his work with a vigor and drive that characterizes his every action and has led his class in scholarship. Now that you have become so firmly fixed in our affections, "Bill," we hate to see you go; nevertheless the Golden West has its charms and will claim you for its own. But say, how about a little jaunt out to Honolulu and Shanghai before we settle down? You're on!

name: THOMAS VIRGIL COLEMAN, Ph.G.
motto: "We have the force within us to overcome our trials, troubles and temptations."
description: Coleman, or better known as "Doc" by his friends, receives his monthly check from the metropolis of Macon, Georgia. "Doc" has a personality that is to be envied. He is stern, straightforward and honest with himself and others. "Doc" bids fair to become the foremost soda jerker of the South. All hail to the future ice cream king! Chocolate soda, please! "Doc" is well read and a good authority on literature. He spends about three hours each night reading suspicious little pink and blue letters. What wondrous knowledge they must contain! Newton's three laws of motion have no effect on him when he is thus engaged. "Doc" is a lover of the diamond and it is said that he twirls the old pill with the ease and grace of a finished ball player. Well, "Doc," the best of friends must part and may old friends ne'er be forgotten. Here's hoping that you make the perilous journey of life without mishap. True friendship is broken only by death.

name: HENRY HARRIS DREWRY, Ph.G.
motto: "So long as it interferes not with my personal welfare, far be it from me to interfere."
description: Argue with me and we shall be friends, agree with me and we cannot agree. During the four years that "Cleo" has been in our midst, he has argued to himself as to whether M.D. should carry him thru life, or whether the art of drugs would be most beneficial. Leaving with "Doc" Wilson's Ph.G., he has not yet decided, but do tell him which you think the best, for he is sure to take the other. However, be this as it may, our tall boy from Griffin, has made good in all that he has attempted. It is quite natural for Harris to have any number of questions asked him during a day from those who do not grasp the difficult problems of Physics and Chemistry. In conclusion, we can truthfully say that he is always willing to lend a helping hand. He will be remembered for time immemorial as one with a true feeling for his fellow man.

name: JOSEPH LEOPOLD GROSS, Ph.G.
motto: "A man pours drugs of which he knows little, into a body of which he knows less."
description: Gross hails from the "Magic City" of Birmingham, Alabama. He has been with us part of two years, having attended part of the time at Auburn, but he has been with us long enough for his classmates to learn to like him, as he is a fine chap, having high ambitions of becoming a "pill-roller." He has an idiosyncrasy of boiling water in a measuring cylinder. In addition to the fact that he likes the physics department, the next of importance is his love for the ladies. He is very popular with the fair sex. "Smoke," as he is known by many of his friends, is a past artist at the popular pastime of "bull slinging," and he should receive his degree for proficiency at same. When seen on the campus he is headed toward Terrell Hall at breakneck speed, carrying under his arm a load of knowledge. Gross, in parting, we bid you adieu with best wishes for a prosperous journey on the Sea of Life.

name: THOMAS JUDSON SASSER, Ph.G.
motto: "Look upward. Press forward. There is much to be done. Help do it."
description: We now have for our "once over" the leader of the Pharmacy class and very deserving is he of that distinction. "T. J." is one of those students with whom "Silence is Golden," but withal he is well known and has a host of friends. He is a hard worker and lets no obstacles hinder the materializing of his purposes. In our mind the greatest tribute that can be paid to a man is that he realizes there is more beyond to do and is willing to help do it. Such a man is "T. J." He does not intend to stop at the completion of his course here and rest upon his laurels, but is going to plunge into the realm of Medicine and further equip himself for a life of service. If you succeed in your profession, "T. J.," as you have succeeded as a student, your cup of reward will be filled to overflowing.

name: James Andrew
motto: "Consistency is a jewel."
description: Look him over, girls, for it may be your last chance to see him as he really is. If you don't like the way that little mustache is parted, you may correspond with the secretary at Vineville Avenue, in the city of Macon, but of course, we couldn't guarantee a personal reply. Jimmie—the Boy Wonder—came to us from Macon, Ga. He attended prep school at Lanier High School, then spent one year in Alabama Polytechnic Institute, but not being satisfied out of the state, he came back to finish his Pre. Med. at Mercer University. After spending three years there and receiving his B.S. Degree, he came to Augusta. Jimmie is the smallest person in the class in statue, but this does not mean that he is below the average mentally, for he is one of the best students in the class, and we predict that he will do great things in the profession he has chosen as his life work.

name: William Jacob Barge
motto: "Virtue alone is the unerring sign of a noble soul."
clubs: Member of Student Council from Senior Class, '22
description: If you don't see why we call him Cupid, you should see him as we had the privilege of seeing him in his surgeon's cap and gown ready to do violence to Willie Stiff. Then perhaps the resemblance to the immortal Dan would be more apparent. Cupid is one of the Mercer boys, having finished three years at that institution before he decided to take up medicine, and came to Georgia for that purpose. His prep school work was done in his home town at the Newnan High School. He has always been a busy man in college since joining the "Immortal thirteen" in 1918, and as member of the Students' Council from the senior class, he has proved his judicial ability. We expect that there will be far fewer crazy folks in the world by 1940 if Cupid takes Psychiatry as we have heard it whispered he possibly will do.

name: ULYSSES SIMPSON BOWEN, B.S., M.D.
motto: "The clock upbraids me with the waste of time."
clubs: Member Students' Council Medical Department University of Georgia from Junior Class, '21, and Senior Class, '22
description: "Useless's" nickname was adopted, as Professor Lustrat would say, "for the sake of euphony alone." In short, it is the condensation of one of his given names, which name was brought into prominence some sixty years ago by one we will take it for granted you remember. Bowen "pre-medded" at Georgia after having "high-schooled" at Metter. Later he showed his appreciation of quality and "medicined" at Georgia, too. "Useless" has not lived up to his nickname at all, for he has proved anything but useless during his course of medical study. Having got wise to the wiles of the flapper shortly after taking up the study of his life-work, he has devoted little attention to the fair sex since, much to the sorrow of said sex. He has been active in student affairs and has a fine record in every way.

name: REESE WATKINS BRADFORD, B.S., M.D.
motto: "'Tis the mind that makes the body rich."
description: "Brad" is an all round good fellow and will do anything in the world he can for you; that is why he was chosen as the most popular student in the Medical College. After graduating at the Georgia Military College and then taking his Pre-Medical work at the University, he came to the Medical College fully prepared to take up the study of medicine. There is no doubt that after he graduates and goes back to the large city of Carlton to minister to all the ills and discomforts of humanity that he will make a big success and in the years to come we will all be proud of the fact that we were in college with him, were associated with him so closely.

name: Claude McKinley Burpee, B.S., M.D.
motto: "Honor lies in honest toil."
enrolltime: Two years in Franklin College and two in the Medical Department
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Class
description: "Burp" is a sure enough Georgia man. He hails from the Classic City, where he attended high school within earshot of the old chapel bell. Having made his farewell to Athens High, he contracted to have his daily routine punctuated by that bell, and listened patiently during two years for its welcome peals to end various and sundry fifty-minute periods, known so well to all Georgia men. Two years in Franklin College and two in the Medical Department gave him his B.S.Med. degree. Now, after having played the game with unwavering determination and marked ability, he has his efforts crowned with the coveted prize, the degree of Doctor of Medicine. "Burp" has had a set objective and has hewed to the line. He has worked and if his college record is any augur of his future as a physician, then let competitors beware. He is quiet, serious and dignified, studious, bright and retentive. He has been a good student and will make a good doctor.

name: Arthur William De Loach, M.D.
motto: "Be silent and safe—silence never betrays you."
description: "Kid" rates with Bowen in being one of the "long boys" of our class. We wonder if long caudal appendages aren't quite an advantage, though, in fording the Harper Street creek and doing the crane stunt down through "Frog Hollow." That reminds us that "Kid" is acknowledged as the king of that district, in spite of his mild disposition, which would preclude at first the possibility of there being any monarchial instincts in him. But of course we refer to his rating with the fair sex, and none can deny him his honor in this particular. "Kid" spent his prep school days at Locust Grove Institute and followed this with his pre-med work at Mercer. His work in Medicine has always been up to the scratch and we expect good things of him when he goes back to Appling to practice.

name: Stacy Claibourne Howell
motto: "An honest man's word is as good as his bond."
description: Stacy is one of the few of our classmates to go through college without a nickname. Although a number of them have been given him they were soon discarded as entirely unsuitable and we know him as just Stacy. Although Stacy stands well enough with the fair sex to have been voted our most popular man with the ladies, he does not let them interfere with his work and so far has been able to avoid all pitfalls. As Editor of the Medical Section of the Pandora, he has proved that we could not have picked a more capable man, for with that as with everything else he goes into, he has put his very best into it. Stacy, like the other Augusta men who prepped at Georgia, is a hard working student, and together with a very active and alert mind, and that wonderful flow of language of his, he is sure to make a success wherever he goes.

name: Plumer Jacobs Manson
motto: "The only way to have a friend is to be one."
major: B.S., M.D., Medical Department of the University
description: "P. J." began his career in the town of Covena, Ga. After taking his prep school at Ocilla High School, he began his college work at Presbyterian College of South Carolina. Graduating from this institution during the stress of the world war he demonstrated his loyalty by enlisting as one of Uncle Sam's "Gobs." After a creditable service in the navy, he entered the Medical Department of the University in the fall of 1918. He has always done his work well, has been active in all branches of student life, and sports the best trimmed little blonde mustache in school. He completed a year as physican at the county home, and if we are to judge by the reports of his charges at that institution, he bids fair to be one of the best dispersers of misery in this part of the country.

name: HENRY GETZEN MEALING, B.S., M.D.
motto: "Measure your mind's height by the shadow it casts."
description: "Hen" may live in South Carolina, but he has lived so close to Georgia all his life that he has become a Georgia man from his heart. After finishing at Richmond Academy at Augusta, he continued his pre-med work at Georgia. Completing his requirements in 1918 he returned to Augusta and entered the Medical Department in the fall of the same year. After weathering the tempestuous assaults of the S. A. T. C. and escaping the culling of the "Grim Reaper," he has now come to be a real M.D. Henry hasn't decided just what kind of a doctor he will be, but he is certain of one thing at least, that he is through with the "stork job" for life. He has always been well liked by his fellow students and members of the faculty, and has always stood above the average in his classes. We are certain that his personality will make him an honored and respected physician in whatever community he decides to practice.

name: RAFAEL FELIX MORALES, P.H.G., M.D.
motto: "On with the dance, let joy be unconfined."
clubs: long-green-holder at all the college dances
description: Rafael has no nickname, unless it is Felix. Felix means happy. Rafael is usually that way. Ever since he cablegrammed his expected arrival from the Gulf of Mexico until the present the pronunciation of his surname has a large interrogation point. It is distinctly not "Morerails." Rafael obtained his bachelor's degree with honors at the Instituto Nacional de Panama, spent a year at the University of Pennsylvania, then entered the Medical Department of the University of Alabama at Mobile. After two successful years there, he came to Georgia and soon proved himself to be "all there." He made quite a name for himself during his junior year as a collector of internal revenue, officiating as long-green-holder at all the college dances. And it was a sly jazzer that got by without having his leg pulled. Rafael is considered by his mentors as one of the best men in his class, and it is with an easy conscience that one may predict for him a brilliant future in his native land.

name: THOMAS CHARLES NASH, B.S.M., M.D.
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."
description: "Cutz" received his preparatory training at Georgia Military College. While there he was known and respected by all, as gentleman, good student and athlete. He entered the University in 1914, received his academic and pre-medical training, and while at the University he retained the honors which he had received at prep school. It was there that he received the name of "Shug" by which he was known for several years. Since entering the Medical College, "Shug" changed the name to "Cutz" and is known by all as "Cutz" Nash. "Cutz" has added a number of friends to his long list. All students and men love and respect him. We understand that he is going back to Philomath to cure the incurable. Luck to you, "Cutz."

name: FRANK McKEMIE ROGERS, M.D.
motto: "I am sick of time and desire to rest."
description: One of our smallest in stature—but brains—that is a different matter. We challenge you to find his equal. And he is as good looking as he is brainy. If you don't believe it just take a look at the accompanying likeness and we are sure that you will agree with us that we have told no falsehood. A friend in need is a friend indeed. This can well be said of Frank, for you will always find him a true friend. Frank hails from Coleman, Georgia. After attending prep school at Locust Grove Institute, then Emory University, he decided that he was too small to do the ordinary toils of life, so picked the profession which his father has followed. Since making this decision and coming to Augusta, he has shown that he is capable of expending untold energy. It is said that Frank is going to return to Coleman and distribute pills to the lucky ones who call him.

name: Frank Griffeth Smith, M.D.
motto: "Nothing is more useful than silence."
enrolltime: after that secured his premedical training in the Classic City. His next step was to enter the Medical Department
description: "Smittie" did his prepping at Mount Carmel Academy and after that secured his premedical training in the Classic City. His next step was to enter the Medical Department and get down to hard work. "Smittie" is such a man as would have pleased Caesar; unlike Cassius, he has not a lean and hungry look; and instead of staying awake at night, he has kept the fair sex awake in their suspense as to who would receive the favors of the favored one. "Smittie" has been a favorite with the unfair sex as well as with those they admire, to-wit: the ladies. And he has been a good boy, notwithstanding the fact that twice he has been the Vice-President of his class. The author of this biography has never even seen him play African golf. It is said he smokes, sometimes in bed. That shows you how luxuriously inclined he is. Even at that, as a young physician, just "watch his smoke."

name: Herbert Steed Alden, B.S. Med.
motto: "Always leave them sighing when you say good bye."
description: There he is, boys, the "Decatur Demon." Herbert has well lived up to this name from his association with the fair sex thruout the state. His well-applied nickname of "Koch" was the result of correspondence with about fifty hospitals in New York for bacteriological work in the summer. The real athlete of the Medical Department is none other than the above gentleman. He makes the "Y" his abode from 6 to 9 P. M. daily. Always true to the honor system, "Koch" is a good student, a good fellow and rather playful at times, his hobby being to skip rope in the dissecting hall. Within another two years the writer predicts that one of the greatest "surgeons" in captivity will graduate from a medical college in the person of our own "Decatur Koch." So here's luck to you in the future.

name: WILLIAM DOUGLAS ANDERSON, B.S.Med.
motto: "I never knew so young a body, so old a head."
description: The Classic City boasts of having the only tree that owns itself, the only double-barrelled cannon, and last but not least, the only man who as a Freshman, beat the Sophs to the "Trimming game." (For details see Athens "Banner" of Sept. 23d, 1917.) Baldy is gifted with the peculiar faculty of making friends, and having once made them, they stick to him, and he to them. On Saturdays and Sundays he usually specializes along these lines, but more particularly with the fair sex, to whom he admits their influences over him. But during the week he is a regular hound for work and at all hours of the night the neighbors can see his light burning as he "sits and ponders" over many of the yet unsolved mysteries of his chosen profession. "Doug's" scholastic record is among the very best. He is a conscientious, hard working student and is possessed with unusual brilliancy. We leave it to him to unveil many of the mysteries of Medicine.

name: THOMAS GIBSON BROOKS, B.S.Med.
motto: "Those who are pleased themselves must always be pleased."
description: Mr. Chairman, before voting upon the motion, allow a word of explanation in behalf of the most handsome man in college. "Tom" has been on trial for four years, and his first and last impression was most favorable. He has a way of his own with the ladies. His proficiency at ballroom wrestling is enviable indeed. "Tom" has always been a dynamic factor in creating and maintaining spirit and good fellowship. His work and play are so interrelated to each other that the greatest benefit is derived from both. He possesses the type of manhood that our Alma Mater is always proud to call her own. We shall remember you, "Tom," as a scholar, as a gentleman, and may your many attainments find expression in your chosen field of endeavor.

name: FELIX BERTEL BROWN
motto: "Singing and dancing alone will not advance one in the world."
description: Some good men have come from the old red hills of Georgia; men whom one likes; men who look one straight in the eye; men who are square in their dealings with their associates; such a type of man is Bert. Socially, Bert is a high stepper, for he strictly adheres to the rule "that when business interferes with pleasure—why, business must be delayed." It is said that Bert's strict adherence to his rule will repay him, for the ladies all like him. Seriously, Bert is a good worker, an intelligent student, and ambitious to accomplish great things. If there be anything in heredity, we predict that Bert's ambition to become a good physician will be realized, for his father and grandfather are physicians. Here's to you, Bert. May you ever be successful.

name: LAURIE LESTER DOZIER
motto: "In friendship I was early taught to believe."
description: Hillman, Georgia, was unheard of until "Slats" came into our midst, but from that time on we have heard nothing else. We are still in doubt as to the exact location of his native hamlet, but some of the boys from Sharon state that it is a suburb of their metropolis. However, we would appreciate some information as to the location of the above mentioned Sharon, Georgia. But we so approve of the first sample from this part of the commonwealth that we would do well to have the county agents work this trade and get us some more of like character. "Legs" has succeeded both in his work as a student, and his unprecedented ability of making friends, for his friends are numbered by his acquaintances, and he makes more each day. In addition to his ability to put out good work in record time, he is about as happy all the time as mere mortal man gets to be, and we aptly designate him "hail fellow, well met." We predict for him all the success that his profession holds for the best, which is the respect of his co-workers, the love of his patients—and a few sheckles on the side!

name: WALDO EMERSON FLOYD
motto: "If you have no pull,—work!"
description: Emerson is a young Ponzi, having a monopoly on anything pertaining to finances. Waldo's efficiency as a Money Collector is so great that he ran the present treasurer of the Freshman class a close race before they could convince the first year men that he was ineligible for that office. He has also acquired in his studies—having gained a much coveted citation from the "Immortal Dick"—along with the respect and admiration of his classmates, a name for being a good all-round student. "Gus" is very impulsive and this trait makes him all the more likeable. "He rushes in where others would fear to venture." This is especially true in his numerous affaires de coeur. As a result of one such "rushing" it is whispered that the climax is drawing near—and in Augusta, too—much to the sorrow of many feminine hearts in Statesboro and other parts of the state. Luck to you, Waldo, and may the best be yours.

name: JULIUS DOAR JOHNSON
motto: "One can attain high levels by trying—even if it takes an aeroplane."
description: "Jule" has so many vantage points from which one could write a pleasing description of him that it is difficult to choose. Probably all his outstanding virtues should be commented upon. First of all, he is a fine fellow, happy, amiable, smiles quickly and easily and wears a brand that won't come off. Next, he is a good student and popular with the faculty and boys. He is quite an artist with a pen, plays the piano and saxophone, is an expert photographer, and please don't omit this—he would rather speed in an automobile or soar in an aeroplane than to eat. He has held creditably important positions in the student organization. One of his pet ambitions is to cover the twenty odd miles between Augusta and Aiken in fifteen minutes flat. It is not true that "Jule" used to play checkers with a certain famous professor during school hours.

name: CHARLES BEATTY KENNEY
motto: "Women are Women and Medicine is Medicine and never the 'twain shall meet."
description: Beatty left the Classic City in 1920 to continue his studies in the Medical Department of his Alma Mater, where he soon acquired the name of "curly-headed Adonis" and not by accident, for seeing is believing. "Ken" has been called "cute" and "darling" by every girl who has met him, but owing to his quiet nature, he has always been unsusceptible to Cupid's darts. However, his fall is predestined since the amourous descent normally occurs at least once in every man's life. His many friends expect quite a crash when the time doth come. He is best known by his consistent work—resulting in his being recognized as one of the best men in the class and bids fair of achieving a wonderful success in his chosen profession. We're for you, Ken.

name: EMMETTE EDWARD MARTIN
motto: "Would not we shatter it to bits,—and then remold it to the heart's desire."
description: The subject of this discussion is Emmette E. Martin, who hails from the metropolis of Hilton, Georgia. Years ago Emmette resolved to quit clodhopping and develop into a notable person along a line which would be of benefit to humanity as well as himself. He is nearing success and in two years from this date will be a full fledged M.D. Emmette is a good, upright sort of man. He can be depended on to always be square and to play the game as becomes a man. He will be found on the right side of most questions, and especially is this true when it comes to questions of a moral nature. Emmette is a good mixer and merrymaker and this side of his nature is never so well brought out as when he is in company of persons of the opposite sex. We believe that success will reward his efforts. Luck to you, Emmette.

name: LELDON WAILS MARTIN
motto: "Never give up, we know what we can do only after we have tried."
description: "Ye crags and peaks; I am with you once again." From the foot of the Blue Ridge Mountains, some moons ago, there came to the University a boy whom we all know by the nickname of "L. W." It is said that the bracing, invigorating air of the high places breeds strong men of character and body. "L. W." has fully maintained this reputation. We know him as a manly fellow, straight in his dealings with his colleagues, honest in purpose, a hard worker in his studies. Always he has stood well in his classes. He was a member of the Phi Kappa Literary Society. In the way of athletics, Martin has accomplished worth while things, for he was a member of the Varsity Track Team and the Sophomore Basketball Team. "L. W.'s" ambition is to be a surgeon, and we predict that he will carve, with his knife, the way to Fame and Fortune.

name: ROBERT CAREY McGAHEE
motto: "The race is neither to the swift, nor the battle to the strong, but time and chance come to all."
description: There is something about the quiet, steady, conscientious, hard working individual which commands and demands the love and respect of his fellow creatures—such a man we affectionately know as "Mac." "Mac" comes from the high level country of McDuffie County, and as we have watched him and known him, we think that he reflects in his quiet steady straightforward manner, the atmosphere of his boyhood home. In his school work, Mac has ever been rated as a good man. He can always be counted upon to be constant in his work, diligent in the pursuit of his studies, and zealous in his desire to accomplish his goal of success. As an impromptu debater in the Demosthenian Literary Society, Mac acquired some degree of fame. It is said that he aspires to be a surgeon. If this be true, we predict for him a success in his chosen profession, and worth while to benefit humanity.

name: Jones Beulah Oliphant
motto: "He doth indeed, show some sparks that are like wit."
description: Oliphant took his high school work in his home town of Wrens and afterwards studied two years at Georgia to get his pre-medical credits. He entered the Medical Department in 1920 and immediately got down to serious study, to which he has been wedded ever since. When given the nickname of "Cocoanut" he took it good naturedly, just as he does all other kidding that comes his way, returning it in kind. And while he has not yet completed his much anticipated book on "How to Speak and Write English Correctly," this failure can be attributed to engagements he has had with Willie Stiff, a well known colored boy that has the habit of getting all cut up, and to other engagements equally engrossing, if not entertaining. You must not judge from Jones' pompadour that he is a ladies' man, for as yet he has seemed to shun the fair sex, but of course he may be taking it out in seeming only. Jones is an all-round good man.

name: Wallace Lamar Poole
motto: "For a woman is only a woman, but a good cigar is a smoke."
description: Wally, another Atlanta boy, originally of Alabama ("and proud of it, sir"), is rare in that he can combine the big 4 in an agreeable ratio, viz., Wine, Women, Waynesboro and Work. His escapades with the fair sex have been numerous, so much so that the "more he sees of the others the less he settles to one." One might think that many of Rudyard's most cynical proverbs were inspired by Wallace—nevertheless, we are confident that all his cynicisms will be forgotten when he meets the real girl, if he has not already, and the indications are to the contrary at the present. Wally, however, is a friend to all, and a hard worker. Wherever he may go he will carry his share of Georgia spirit. Au revoir, Wallace L.

name: ALBERT HENRY POWELL
motto: "It is fortune, not wisdom, that rules man's life."
description: "Al" is another boy from A. R. C. who has certainly lived up to the ideals of that famous institution which is known to be one of the best prep schools in the South. Ever since "P. A." has been in college he has been a thorough and conscientious student, but not the bookworm type, since he is always ready to go out "amongst them," and does quite often, but not enough to keep phone calls from chasing him during the day. Some sage has written, "Men are of two kinds—the kind women run after and the kind women run from." Al is of the former type. Characteristic of Albert is his pipe, which is quite famous, if such an inanimate object as a pipe can be called famous. It all happened on a mixed camp last summer. Said pipe being smoked as an antidote for moonlight intoxication by all the girls. Needless to say, it is one of his priceless possessions now. 'Cess to you, Al.

name: THOMAS PORTER REVILLE
motto: "Give every man thy ear, but few thy voice."
description: They say that Reville is not much in a crowd—but when he is in the Okeefeenokee swamp—oh! Just give him a gun, a box of shells, his old pipe and worlds of tobacco, and dump him off in the wild swamp, and to him then, the "world is a Paradise now." Before Reville was sixteen years of age, he had, according to the newspaper reports, proved himself to be a chip off the old block, for he had killed and bagged three black bears. However, those of us who have been permitted to know Reville more intimately know that he has "other ideas in his head" besides those of hunting. He is studying Medicine, and we know that his calm, steady, even disposition, his excellent brain, his conscientious spirit will all unite to the benefit of many a poor old frame which has been racked in pain and distress. Porter, here's to you—we like you.

name: DAVID STEINBERG, B.S.Med.
motto: "Could I love less, I should be happier now."
description: From Augusta several moons ago there came to the University one whom we all have learned to know as a most likeable boy—he answers to the nickname of "Dave." Dave's friendship is not limited to the male sex, for a great amount of his time is allotted to the ladies and it is said that they like his winning smile, so much so that he is regarded as a dangerous rival. In class work, Dave has always stood well and he can be counted upon to be constant, eager, diligent, and zealous in the pursuit of knowledge. The field is never too crowded for a good man, and even in the Medical profession the best reach the top. Dave is studying medicine and we predict for him a brilliant future. Luck to you, "Dave." We are counting upon you.

name: BENJAMIN ARTHUR WILKINSON, B.S.Med.
motto: "To work for the pleasure there is in resting."
description: "Pewee" hails from Quitman, Georgia, and admits it himself. He entered into our midst during the term of Nineteen and readily gained the enviable position of leader of the class, as well as a host of friends. He is a man worthy of anyone's friendship and respect, being strictly honest and upright in every dealing, as well as one who lives a life above reproach, a gentleman in the true sense. "Wilk's" one big fault and besetting sin is his admiration for the fair sex, and he will stray from the path of duty now and then and neglect his work for a few hours pleasure with one of his many admirers. This is a case of a man made for a profession, for "Wilk" will fill every requirement of a physician, as he is serious and conscientious, and possesses the necessary qualities which lead to success in any field. The medical profession needs good men, and we predict a rapid and brilliant future for "Wilk" in his chosen field, and expect to see him at the top in a short while after beginning his life work.

name: PERINO BOONE WINGFIELD, B.S.Med.
motto: "If your lips keep from slips, of these five things beware, of whom you speak, to whom you speak, and how and when and where."
enrolltime: three years in the University as a pre-med student
description: "P. B." hails from the Classic City where he received his preparation for medical college from the first grade thru the high school and then three years in the University as a pre-med student. Like the well known Cassius, this noble son of Georgia's northland has a "lean and hungry look," but this is as far as the comparison may go. Wingfield is a good all round scout doing equally well in his mental endeavor as in his flirtation with the fairer sex. He has numerous female admirers and quite a number of male associates, who, more or less, think quite a bit of him. His excellent standing in the Medical College earned him membership in the Skull and Bones which in itself is quite an attainment as far as college honors go. There is little use to predict big things for this mental monstrosity, for every one believes he will succeed in his chosen duty. However, considering his past record, we believe that we are safe to say that he will have a large and lucrative female practice. Luck to you, Perrino!

name: ALICE KLEMENTINE CHANDLER
motto: "Beauty is truth, truth beauty—that is all you know on earth, and all ye need to know."
description: When Alice was a student at Lucy Cobb she was voted the best looking girl in school, and since that time her looks haven't changed for the worse. She was also one of the best students in her class. After receiving Miss Millie's blessing, Alice tried Gunstan Hall for a time, but she decided that Athens was the place for her and came back to be a Co-ed. Her work at the University has been a credit to her former institution; her marks are always above the average. Furthermore, despite the keenest competition, Alice holds the record of having come in late to more classes than any other "town girl" Co-ed. In other words, Alice is all right. She dances or attends classes or entertains with a cheerfulness and earnestness that commands everybody's friendship. There is just one person you would like better than Alice, and that's her mother.

name: MARY RICHARDS COLVIN
motto: "Interpret life thru its flower."
enrolltime: Entering the University as a Sophomore
clubs: Member Y. W. C. A. Cabinet, '20, '21
description: "Dick," as she is called by her many friends on the Campus, is an all-round girl. Her talents extend from the Rifle Range to the Law School. Entering the University as a Sophomore with the first of the Co-eds, she took an outstanding place among them. She has entered all phases of college life, and in each has stood out prominently. She has the honor of being the first woman to enter the Lumpkin Law School and the first one to register in Franklin College for an A.B. degree. She has a personality which makes and holds many friends. She is a good student, a strong leader and a splendid girl. Though we are not prophets, we know that she will be successful in anything she undertakes. We say this because we know her.
